engine.name: GameEngine
engine.stageCompletion: 100% for the current core-first-mvp scope; not a commercial-engine completion claim.
stageClosureNotes: MVP closure is validated through the source-tree default build/test/agent contract plus documented optional desktop runtime and package lanes.
Apple/iOS/Metal validation remains host-gated and is not counted as complete without macOS/Xcode or CI evidence.
Generated-game, sample-game, desktop runtime, and desktop package readiness are limited to the validation recipes listed in this manifest.

languagePolicy.modules: Use CMake FILE_SET CXX_MODULES for project modules.
languagePolicy.importStd: Allowed only when CMake reports C++23 standard-library module support for the active generator/toolchain.

commands.removeMergedWorktree: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> [-BaseRef origin/main] [-BaseBranch main] [-Remote origin] [-LocalCheckoutPath <path>] [-DeleteLocalBranch]

windowsDiagnosticsToolchain.policy: Official Microsoft host diagnostics only. These tools are not default build dependencies, are not installed by CMake configure, and are blockers only for tasks that explicitly need native debugging, D3D12 debug-layer validation, GPU capture, or ETW/performance evidence.

debuggingToolsForWindows.purpose: Native crash, dump, cdb, and windbg debugging for the Windows/MSVC lane.

graphicsTools.purpose: D3D12 debug layer support for Windows backend diagnostics.

pixOnWindows.purpose: D3D12 GPU captures, timing, and counter investigations.

windowsPerformanceToolkit.purpose: ETW tracing plus CPU and system performance investigations.

windowsDiagnosticsToolchain.hostGatePolicy: Missing Debugging Tools for Windows, Windows Graphics Tools, PIX on Windows, or Windows Performance Toolkit must be reported as a host diagnostics blocker only for the relevant diagnostic task; do not report it as an engine compile, CMake, or default validation failure.

modules.name: MK_assets
modules.purpose: Logical asset ids, asset records, asset registry, and Asset Identity v2 foundation through AssetKeyV2 rows, deterministic validation diagnostics, stable AssetId derivation from keys, and deterministic GameEngine.AssetIdentity.v2 text IO. Also provides GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 source asset registry validation, deterministic text IO, Asset Identity v2 projection, and texture/mesh/audio/material/scene import metadata projection without importer execution, cooked artifact writes, .geindex package updates, renderer/RHI residency, or package streaming. Also provides asset dependency graph, hot reload snapshot/event tracking, debounce-based recook request scheduling with dependency invalidation, failed-reload rollback state, runtime asset replacement staging with safe-point commits, recook import plan extraction, cooked package index serialization with content hashes, dependency edges, and deterministic recook decisions including ui_atlas package records and ui_atlas_texture dependency edges, canonical cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 metadata validation/serialization through UiAtlasMetadataDocument and UiAtlasMetadataGlyph without source image decoding, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, renderer texture upload, or production atlas packing, deterministic cooked GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata validation/serialization through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture dependency rows while keeping source decoding, production atlas packing, and native GPU sprite batching unsupported, shader source metadata, generated shader artifact tracking, shader descriptor reflection rows, shader compiler command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shell-free shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, deterministic shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, texture/mesh/audio/material/scene import metadata, scene mesh/material/sprite dependency edge kinds, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document decoding including optional deterministic hex byte payloads, GameEngine.MorphMeshCpuSource.v1 morph mesh CPU source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedMorphMeshCpu.v1 cook field emission, GameEngine.AnimationFloatClipSource.v1 animation float clip source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedAnimationFloatClip.v1 cook field emission through the asset import tool, GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 3D TRS quaternion clip source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip.v1 cook field emission, AssetKind::animation_quaternion_clip, and animation_quaternion_clip import metadata planning, GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource.v1 source document validation and deterministic text IO through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument, serialize_animation_transform_binding_source_document, and deserialize_animation_transform_binding_source_document for curve-target to named transform component authoring without scene-node resolution or runtime payload cooking, deterministic asset import planning, material definition serialization, GameEngine.MaterialGraph.v1 graph-authoring IR with deterministic text IO, validation diagnostics, and lowering to MaterialDefinition for the minimal v1 node set (graph_output, constant factors, texture nodes) without shader graph generation, GameEngine.MaterialGraphShaderExport.v0 bridge text IO plus deterministic HLSL stub emission for reviewed DXC planning without automatic package cook of export documents, material instance serialization/composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, and explicit backend-neutral material binding metadata with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and sampler rows. Asset Identity v2 is implemented foundation-only, and Source Asset Registry v1 does not complete scene/render/UI/gameplay reference cleanup, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, or 2D/3D playable vertical slices. External PNG decoded RGBA8 payloads, glTF position-only and interleaved position+normal+uv+tangent mesh payload conversion, common-audio decoded PCM payloads, and first-party GameEngine.Scene.v1 source validation/canonicalization are owned by MK_tools adapters so runtime/game code remains cooked-artifact-only.

Z-rotation-only radians, joint-track bindings, deterministic pose diagnostics, local pose sampling with missing tracks falling back to rest pose for gameplay, scene, and editor tests, Animation Quaternion Local Pose Foundation v1 with AnimationJointLocalTransform3d, AnimationSkeleton3dDesc, AnimationPose3d, AnimationModelPose3d, validate_animation_skeleton_3d_desc, validate_animation_pose_3d, make_animation_rest_pose_3d, and build_animation_model_pose_3d for unit-quaternion local rotation storage and model-pose matrices, Animation Quaternion Clip Sampling AnimationJointTrack3dByteSource, and make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes for normalized shortest-path quaternion keyframe sampling, cooked byte-row decoding, and selected 3D local-pose track application, Animation Skin Payload Foundation v0 with skin joint palette entries, per-vertex influence rows, deterministic skin diagnostics, influence normalization, model-space joint matrices, and skinning palette construction, Animation CPU Skinning Foundation v0 with AnimationCpuSkinningDesc validation and skin_animation_vertices_cpu for deterministic skinned-position output from bind positions, skin influence rows, and palette matrices, Animation CPU Skinned Normal Stream v1 with optional bind_normals validation plus deterministic skinned-normal output, Animation CPU Skinned Tangent Stream v1 with optional bind_tangents validation plus deterministic skinned-tangent output, Animation CPU Skinned Bitangent Handedness v1 with optional bind_tangent_handedness validation plus deterministic skinned-bitangent reconstruction from skinned normal/tangent rows, AnimationMorphMeshCpuDesc and AnimationMorphTargetCpuDesc with validate_animation_morph_mesh_cpu_desc, morph targets, AnimationMorphWeightsTrackDesc with validate_animation_morph_weights_track_desc and sample_animation_morph_weights_at_time for LINEAR mesh weight channels with per-target weights in [0,1], planar two-bone IK through AnimationTwoBoneIkXyBendSide, AnimationTwoBoneIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, and solve_animation_two_bone_ik_xy_plane with explicit bend-side plus finite non-collinear pole-vector branch selection, analytic two-bone 3D orientation basis output through AnimationTwoBoneIk3dDesc, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dJointFrame, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dSolution, and AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, AnimationFabrikIkXySegmentRotationLimit, AnimationFabrikIkXySolution, solve_animation_fabrik_ik_xy_chain, AnimationIkLocalRotationLimit, and Backward accumulation, negative-time wrapping, inferred clip duration, tangent-space material/render integration beyond the cooked tangent-frame schema, broader IK constraints beyond the implemented planar segment local Z and 3D swing/twist local clamps, broader generated-game animation/morph integration, async compute scheduling, graphics morph+skin composition, Metal compute morph parity, broad GPU skinning backend parity beyond the recorded D3D12/Vulkan package evidence, and animation graph authoring remain follow-up systems.

modules.name: MK_ai
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only gameplay decision-flow foundation. Behavior Tree Foundation v0 provides value-type BehaviorTreeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeDesc, BehaviorTreeLeafResult, BehaviorTreeTickResult, diagnostics, and visited-node traces for deterministic memoryless sequence, selector, action, and condition evaluation. Behavior Tree Blackboard Conditions v0 adds typed BehaviorTreeBlackboardValue rows for bool, signed integer, finite double, and string facts, deterministic BehaviorTreeBlackboard snapshots, BehaviorTreeBlackboardCondition bindings, and BehaviorTreeEvaluationContext so condition nodes can read caller-supplied blackboard snapshots while action nodes still use game-supplied leaf statuses. AI Perception Services v1 adds AiPerceptionAgent2D, AiPerceptionTarget2D, AiPerceptionSnapshot2D, build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, and write_ai_perception_blackboard for explicit caller-supplied 2D sight/hearing classification, stable primary-target selection, duplicate target rejection, finite validation, and blackboard projection into caller-selected fact keys. It reports missing-root, duplicate-node-id, missing-child, empty-composite, leaf-has-children, missing-leaf-result, invalid-leaf-result, duplicate-leaf-result, duplicate-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-value, duplicate-blackboard-condition, invalid-blackboard-condition, missing-blackboard-key, blackboard-type-mismatch, invalid-blackboard-operator, invalid-agent, invalid-target, duplicate-target-id, invalid-perception-blackboard-key, calculation-overflow, cycle, and max-depth diagnostics without owning async tasks, decorators/services, scene state, physics queries, navigation requests, persistent blackboard services, editor graph authoring, platform APIs, middleware, scripting, or third-party dependencies.

modules.name: MK_audio
modules.purpose: Device-independent audio foundations. Currently provides an AudioMixer that validates audio buses, voices, spatial listener/source descriptors, clip metadata, device render requests, resampling quality, and interleaved float clip sample data; tracks logical clip asset ids; produces deterministic mix commands; builds spatial voice/listener metadata for distance attenuation and stereo pan; plans sample-frame voice scheduling; records streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, and underrun diagnostics; provides AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state without codecs; renders deterministic interleaved float PCM buffers with gain, selectable nearest/linear resampling, channel conversion, and clamping; exposes AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float so host adapters can fill output queues from AudioMixer PCM using bounded render chunks; and does not open OS audio devices.

modules.name: MK_audio_sdl3
modules.purpose: Optional SDL3 desktop audio adapter. Currently provides SdlAudioRuntime for SDL_INIT_AUDIO lifetime and SdlAudioDevice for default playback streams through SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream, pause/resume/clear controls, queued-frame reporting, silence queuing, and interleaved float PCM queuing while keeping SDL stream and device handles private.

modules.name: MK_math
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only 2D/3D math primitives for games and engine systems, including vectors, matrices, unit quaternions, quaternion vector rotation, Mat4::rotation_quat, and transforms with position, scale, and radians-based rotation.

modules.name: MK_core
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only deterministic engine core. Currently provides GameApp and headless runner lifecycle contracts, logging contracts, fixed timestep behavior, entity ids, component registry, version data, and dependency-free diagnostics/counter/profile capture plus Trace Event JSON subset export through DiagnosticEvent, CounterSample, ProfileSample, DiagnosticCapture, DiagnosticSummary, ManualProfileClock, SteadyProfileClock, DiagnosticsRecorder, ScopedProfileZone, summarize_diagnostics, DiagnosticsTraceExportOptions, export_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsOpsHostStatus, DiagnosticsOpsPlanOptions, DiagnosticsOpsArtifact, DiagnosticsOpsPlan, and build_diagnostics_ops_plan without exposing runtime, renderer, platform, editor, SDL3, OS, GPU, network upload, or Dear ImGui APIs.

modules.name: MK_ui_renderer
modules.purpose: First-party runtime UI renderer adapter that consumes MK_ui RendererSubmission payloads through the existing MK_renderer public API without making MK_ui depend on renderer code. Currently provides UiRendererTheme token-to-color mapping, resolves styled UI boxes to renderer colors, converts RendererBox bounds into SpriteCommand rectangles, resolves deterministic MonospaceTextLayoutPolicy glyph rows through UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette and UiRendererGlyphAtlasBinding by font_family plus glyph id into atlas-backed text glyph sprite submissions, resolves ImageAdapterPayload rows through UiRendererImagePalette and UiRendererImageBinding into image sprite submissions with deterministic resource_id-first and asset_uri-fallback lookup, can attach first-party cooked atlas page and UV metadata for glyph and image bindings, can build UiRendererImagePalette rows from MK_runtime RuntimeUiAtlasPayload package metadata for the native-ui-atlas-package-metadata proof, can build glyph palette rows from RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph package metadata through build_ui_renderer_glyph_atlas_palette_from_runtime_ui_atlas for the Runtime UI Glyph Atlas Package Bridge v1 proof, submits styled UI boxes, resolved text glyphs, and resolved image placeholders through IRenderer, reports text run, text adapter row, text glyph availability/resolution/missing/submission, image placeholder, image sprite submission, missing image resource, accessibility node, and adapter diagnostic availability, and leaves concrete text shaping/font loading/rasterization/glyph atlas generation/source image decoding/production atlas packing/renderer texture upload/OS accessibility bridges behind their separate MK_ui adapter boundaries.

modules.name: MK_platform
modules.purpose: Platform-facing contracts. Currently provides VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput for mouse/touch/pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput for gamepad buttons and axes, DisplayInfo and WindowDisplayState for monitor/DPI/safe-area metadata plus deterministic monitor selection and window placement policy without native handles, mobile lifecycle/orientation/safe-area/touch/storage/permission contracts for Android and Apple adapters, IClipboard and MemoryClipboard for text clipboard workflows, FileDialogRequest/IFileDialogService/MemoryFileDialogService for asynchronous native-style open/save/folder dialog workflows, CursorMode/ICursor/MemoryCursor for cursor visibility, confinement, and relative-input state, VirtualLifecycle for quit, termination, low-memory, background, and foreground lifecycle events, HeadlessWindow, MemoryFileSystem, RootedFileSystem, file watch backend selection with native backend family reporting, deterministic PollingFileWatcher file event scanning, WindowsFileWatcher backed by Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW event scanning behind PIMPL, LinuxFileWatcher backed by inotify event scanning, MacOSFileWatcher backed by FSEvents event scanning, host-unavailable native watcher diagnostics, shell-free ProcessCommand validation, a recording process runner, and a Windows CreateProcessW runner that captures stdout, stderr, exit code, and launch diagnostics without exposing native handles.

modules.name: MK_runtime_rhi
modules.purpose: Backend-neutral bridge from typed runtime payloads to RHI resources. Currently creates MK_rhi texture resources from RuntimeTexturePayload with owner-device provenance, writes cooked bytes into upload buffers, records copy-command upload paths when cooked texture byte payloads are present, creates vertex/index RHI buffers from RuntimeMeshPayload byte payloads with buffer-copy upload paths, uploads RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload POSITION deltas into storage buffers plus 256-byte-backed weight uniforms through upload_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu, make_runtime_morph_mesh_gpu_binding, and attach_morph_mesh_descriptor_set, exposes RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBinding plus create_runtime_morph_mesh_compute_binding for the narrow D3D12 compute morph proof that binds base POSITION bytes, POSITION deltas, morph weights, a compute-write output POSITION buffer, and opt-in NORMAL/TANGENT output buffers/descriptors when uploaded morph normal/tangent streams are present without native handles, exposes make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding to map a vertex-usage compute output POSITION buffer plus the original index buffer into renderer MeshGpuBinding for the D3D12 renderer-consumption proof, derives position-only float32x3 stride 12 and lit tangent-space stride 48 (position, normal, TEXCOORD_0, tangent vec4 with handedness in w) vertex layout contracts from cooked mesh metadata, rejects partial normal/UV/tangent-frame layouts before buffer creation even when manual upload options are used, produces renderer MeshGpuBinding payloads through make_runtime_mesh_gpu_binding with owner-device validation data, keeps texture metadata-only payloads explicit while mesh uploads require bytes, derives material descriptor set layout descriptions from set-0 material binding metadata, and promotes MaterialFactors plus optional caller-owned descriptor set layouts into descriptor sets, 256-byte-backed uniform buffers carrying the 64-byte material factor payload, sampled texture descriptor writes, sampler descriptor writes, and Runtime RHI Upload Submission Fence Rows v1 submitted mirakana::rhi::FenceValue rows for texture, mesh, skinned mesh, morph mesh, and material-factor uploads with owner-device validation without exposing native backend handles or claiming native async upload execution.

modules.name: MK_runtime_scene_rhi
modules.purpose: Backend-neutral bridge from cooked runtime scene packages to renderer-visible scene GPU binding palettes. Currently builds retained SceneGpuBindingPalette resources from RuntimeAssetPackage plus SceneRenderPacket, uploads unique referenced mesh, skinned mesh, texture, and material payloads through MK_runtime_rhi, can explicitly select cooked morph_mesh_cpu package rows through RuntimeSceneGpuBindingOptions::morph_mesh_assets and retain them as SceneMorphGpuBindingPalette resources with a shared morph descriptor set layout, preserves derived mesh layout metadata such as position-only stride 12 and lit tangent-space stride 48 in renderer-visible MeshGpuBinding values, retains mesh uploads, skinned mesh uploads, morph mesh uploads, texture uploads, material descriptor bindings, generated material pipeline layouts, and actual submit-order RuntimeSceneGpuBindingResult::submitted_upload_fences rows in the result, exposes execute_runtime_scene_gpu_upload and RuntimeSceneGpuUploadExecutionReport for host-owned upload execution status with backend-neutral mesh/skinned/morph/texture/material counts, uploaded byte totals, submitted_upload_fence_count, and last_submitted_upload_fence rows derived from that ordered fence list, exposes execute_runtime_scene_gpu_binding_safe_point_teardown for host-owned safe-point GPU teardown handoff with deterministic null-device handle invalidation plus explicit host-native destroy guidance for non-null IRhiDevice implementations without exposing native handles to gameplay, uses one scene-shared material pipeline layout, supports caller-owned material descriptor set layouts while creating a matching pipeline layout itself, rejects incompatible default material descriptor layouts for the current single-pipeline RhiFrameRenderer path, reports deterministic per-asset failures, and keeps MK_runtime, MK_scene, SDL3, native window handles, concrete RHI backends, Dear ImGui, and editor APIs out of generated gameplay code.

modules.name: MK_runtime_scene
modules.purpose: Renderer-free cooked runtime scene gameplay bridge. Runtime Scene Gameplay Instance v0 provides mirakana::runtime_scene::instantiate_runtime_scene, RuntimeSceneInstance, RuntimeSceneReference, RuntimeSceneDiagnostic, RuntimeSceneLoadOptions, RuntimeSceneLoadResult, and find_runtime_scene_nodes_by_name so generated games and engine tests can instantiate a cooked RuntimeAssetPackage scene payload into mirakana::Scene state, enumerate mesh/material/sprite references, validate missing or wrong-kind referenced assets, optionally report duplicate node names, and receive deterministic diagnostics for missing scene assets, wrong scene asset kind, malformed scene payloads, and deserialization failures. Runtime Scene Animation Transform Binding v1 adds resolve_runtime_scene_animation_transform_bindings and apply_runtime_scene_animation_transform_samples to resolve GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource.v1 rows against RuntimeSceneInstance node names and apply caller-provided FloatAnimationCurveSample rows to scene node transforms through MK_animation validation, rejecting invalid binding documents, missing node names, and duplicate node names before mutation. Runtime Scene Quaternion Animation Transform Binding v1 adds apply_runtime_scene_animation_pose_3d to resolve AnimationSkeleton3dDesc joint names against RuntimeSceneInstance node names and apply validated AnimationPose3d translation, unit-quaternion rotation converted into the current Transform3D Euler storage, and scale rows before mutation. It does not sample curves from time, add animation graphs, add package formats, add skeletal renderer deformation, expand scalar binding rows beyond translation xyz / rotation z / scale xyz, or depend on MK_renderer, MK_rhi, MK_scene_renderer, editor, platform windows, SDL3, Dear ImGui, or native handles.

modules.name: MK_physics
modules.purpose: Deterministic gameplay physics foundations. Currently provides PhysicsWorld2D and PhysicsWorld3D with validated body descriptions, force accumulation, gravity, damping, fixed-step-friendly integration, deterministic 2D/3D sweep-and-prune broadphase pairs, 2D AABB/circle contacts, 3D AABB/sphere/capsule contacts, collision layer/mask filtering, deterministic collision-bounds raycasts with nearest-hit selection and ray mask filtering, trigger body flags plus non-resolving trigger overlap reporting, conservative bounds-based PhysicsShapeSweep2DDesc and PhysicsShapeSweep3DDesc shape sweeps with nearest-hit, ignored-body, trigger-inclusion, mask, and initial-overlap handling, deterministic exact 3D primitive sweeps through PhysicsShape3DDesc::aabb, PhysicsShape3DDesc::sphere, PhysicsShape3DDesc::capsule, PhysicsQueryFilter3D, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DDesc, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DHit, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DResult, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DStatus, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DDiagnostic, and PhysicsWorld3D::exact_shape_sweep against current AABB/sphere/capsule target primitives with nearest-hit, deterministic epsilon tie-breaking, initial-overlap, disabled-body, ignored-body, trigger-inclusion, mask, conservative false-positive rejection, and invalid-request diagnostics, plus the first-class PhysicsExactSphereCast3DDesc, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DHit, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DResult, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DStatus, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DDiagnostic, and PhysicsWorld3D::exact_sphere_cast convenience wrapper. deterministic contact manifolds through PhysicsContactPoint3D, PhysicsContactManifold3D, and PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds with stable feature ids, warm-start-safe point rows, trigger/disabled exclusion, and solver consumption for current AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule contacts. PhysicsContinuousStep3DConfig, PhysicsContinuousStep3DRow, PhysicsContinuousStep3DResult, and PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous expose opt-in deterministic 3D CCD rows for fast dynamic AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule bodies swept against current non-trigger AABB/sphere/capsule targets while PhysicsWorld3D::step remains discrete. PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DDesc, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DRowKind, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DRow, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DResult, and evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d expose pure non-mutating generated-game push, step-up, slope, grounded, trigger, reciprocal layer/mask, and dynamic-body policy rows over the current query/contact/CCD surface. PhysicsJoint3DStatus, PhysicsJoint3DDiagnostic, PhysicsDistanceJoint3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DConfig, PhysicsJointSolve3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DRow, PhysicsJointSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_joints_3d expose explicit first-party distance-joint solving with deterministic diagnostics, replay rows, invalid-request rejection, disabled-joint rows, velocity preservation, and no persistent world joint storage. PhysicsDeterminismGate3DStatus, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DDiagnostic, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DConfig, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DCounts, PhysicsReplaySignature3D, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DResult, make_physics_replay_signature_3d, and evaluate_physics_determinism_gate_3d expose host-independent replay signatures and count-based budget gates over current 3D bodies, broadphase pairs, contacts, manifolds, trigger overlaps, and contact points. Iterative contact solver configuration with correction slop/percent controls, stacked-body coverage, replay-tested inverse-mass contact resolution, PhysicsCharacterController3DDesc and move_physics_character_controller_3d for conservative capsule movement/contact/grounding rows over PhysicsWorld3D::shape_sweep, PhysicsAuthoredCollisionScene3DDesc and build_physics_world_3d_from_authored_collision_scene for reviewed in-memory collision body rows with native backend requests failing closed, and a validated Physics 1.0 ready surface with explicit Jolt/native middleware exclusion and future optional-adapter gates is implemented. Dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, vehicles, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends outside the 1.0 ready surface, middleware, oriented boxes, mesh/convex casts, timing benchmarks, and broad physics beyond the first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface remain follow-up work.

modules.name: MK_navigation
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only deterministic navigation foundation for generated games and gameplay tests. Currently provides NavigationGrid, NavigationGridCoord, NavigationPathRequest, NavigationPathResult, NavigationPathStatus, and find_navigation_path for bounds-checked cardinal grid A* pathfinding with walkability, positive traversal costs, stable tie-breaking, invalid/blocked endpoint diagnostics, no-path reporting, total path cost, and visited-node counts. Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 adds NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering for grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit path-follow cursor advancement, max-step/arrival-radius movement, remaining-distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering. Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 adds NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent for value-type idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid movement state, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, and desired velocity from actual path movement. Grid Dynamic Obstacle Replan v0 adds NavigationGridPathValidationRequest, NavigationGridPathValidationResult, NavigationGridPathValidationStatus, validate_navigation_grid_path, NavigationGridReplanRequest, NavigationGridReplanResult, NavigationGridReplanStatus, and replan_navigation_grid_path for validating remaining cardinal-grid paths against the current grid, reporting empty/start/goal/out-of-bounds/blocked/non-cardinal/unsupported-adjacency/cost-overflow diagnostics, reusing valid remaining paths, and recalculating deterministic replacement paths around newly blocked cells through find_navigation_path. Navigation Agent Local Avoidance v0 adds NavigationLocalAvoidanceRequest, NavigationLocalAvoidanceResult, NavigationLocalAvoidanceDiagnostic, and calculate_navigation_local_avoidance for finite-input single-agent velocity adjustment against explicit neighbor rows, duplicate-neighbor and calculation-overflow diagnostics, deterministic neighbor ordering, closest-approach prediction over the requested time window, overlap/future-collision separation, and max-speed clamping. Navigation Grid Path Smoothing v0 adds NavigationGridPathSmoothingRequest, NavigationGridPathSmoothingResult, NavigationGridPathSmoothingStatus, and smooth_navigation_grid_path for reducing validated cardinal grid paths through deterministic walkable line-of-sight while preserving start and goal waypoints. Navigation Production Path v1 adds NavigationGridAgentPathRequest, NavigationGridAgentPathPlan, NavigationGridAgentPathStatus, NavigationGridAgentPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_grid_agent_path to compose validated grid pathfinding, optional smoothing, point-path mapping, and NavigationAgentState creation for generated-game route setup. navmesh assets, polygon navigation, spline/funnel smoothing, full crowd simulation, RVO/ORCA, scene/physics integration, editor visualization, middleware, and background navigation jobs remain future gameplay-system slices.

modules.name: MK_platform_sdl3
modules.purpose: Optional SDL3 platform backend with RAII runtime/window wrappers, SDL display enumeration and first-party monitor selection mapped into DisplayInfo, SDL window position/size movement mapped through WindowPlacement, SDL window display scale, pixel density, and safe-area mapping into WindowDisplayState, SDL text clipboard mapping into IClipboard and SdlClipboardTextAdapter mapping SdlClipboard through MK_ui IClipboardTextAdapter, SDL asynchronous native file dialogs mapped into IFileDialogService, SDL cursor visibility/grab/relative mode mapping into ICursor, SdlPlatformIntegrationAdapter mapping validated MK_ui PlatformTextInputRequest begin/end rows to SDL_SetTextInputArea plus SDL_StartTextInput/SDL_StopTextInput for caller-owned SdlWindow instances, SDL text input, text editing, and text editing candidate events copied into first-party SdlWindowEvent committed-text, composition-text/range, candidate-string, selected-candidate, candidate-list-orientation, SDL keycode, and key-modifier rows without retaining SDL-owned text or candidate pointers, selected SDL3 Ctrl/GUI + C/X/V key-down rows mapped through sdl3_text_edit_clipboard_command_from_window_event / apply_sdl3_text_edit_clipboard_command_event into MK_ui TextEditClipboardCommand application through IClipboardTextAdapter, SDL app lifecycle events mapped into VirtualLifecycle, SDL key mapping into VirtualInput, SDL mouse/touch mapping into VirtualPointerInput, and SDL gamepad button/axis/device mapping into VirtualGamepadInput without exposing SDL_Window through MK_ui.

modules.name: MK_runtime_host
modules.purpose: Editor-independent desktop game host contract. Currently provides DesktopGameRunner, DesktopRunConfig, DesktopRunResult, DesktopHostServices, and IDesktopEventPump so games can keep implementing mirakana::GameApp while a host drives IWindow lifecycle, VirtualInput, VirtualPointerInput, VirtualGamepadInput, VirtualLifecycle, optional renderer resize including swapchain-backed RhiFrameRenderer resize through IRenderer, optional DiagnosticsRecorder/IProfileClock frame instrumentation, backend-neutral renderer counter capture from IRenderer::stats(), and app update/stop status without exposing SDL, OS, RHI, or graphics API handles to game code. It also provides load_desktop_shader_bytecode_pair for host-independent packaged shader bytecode loading through IFileSystem with owned byte vectors, entry points, stable statuses, and diagnostics.

modules.name: MK_runtime_host_sdl3
modules.purpose: Optional SDL3 desktop game runtime host adapter. Currently provides SdlDesktopEventPump, which owns the SDL_PollEvent loop in the adapter implementation and forwards translated SDL events through SdlWindow::handle_event into first-party virtual input, pointer, gamepad, lifecycle, and window contracts. Game code includes engine headers only and does not include SDL3 headers or native handles.

modules.name: MK_runtime_host_sdl3_presentation
modules.purpose: Optional SDL3 desktop game presentation and reusable game-host adapter. Provides SdlDesktopGameHost, which owns SDL runtime/window setup, event pumping, SdlDesktopPresentation renderer selection, virtual input/pointer/gamepad/lifecycle state, and DesktopGameRunner service wiring for games that select the desktop-game-runtime lane without exposing SDL headers, native OS handles, GPU handles, RHI backend handles, Dear ImGui, or editor APIs to gameplay code. Provides SdlDesktopPresentation, which keeps SDL3 native window-property probing, D3D12/Vulkan-ready first-party surface candidate creation, host-supplied precompiled D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V shader bytecode renderer creation, host-owned D3D12/Vulkan scene GPU binding injection with request vertex input validated against referenced cooked mesh layouts, host-owned D3D12/Vulkan Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 renderer creation from target-specific postprocess shader artifacts plus optional scene-depth input, host-owned Package-Visible Directional Shadow Filtering v0 creation from target-specific shadow receiver/shadow shader artifacts with fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF metadata, renderer selection diagnostics, postprocess diagnostics, postprocess_depth_input_requested/postprocess_depth_input_ready report fields, directional_shadow_status/directional_shadow_requested/directional_shadow_ready/directional_shadow_filter_mode/directional_shadow_filter_tap_count/directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels report fields, package-visible renderer quality gate reporting through evaluate_sdl_desktop_presentation_quality_gate and renderer_quality_* fields, and NullRenderer fallback policy in host code while exposing only first-party mirakana:: diagnostics, RHI value-type descriptors, SdlDesktopPresentationReport status fields, SdlDesktopPresentationQualityGateReport summary fields, and IRenderer access to the host.

modules.name: MK_rhi
modules.purpose: Graphics-API-independent RHI contracts for backend capability/probe profiles, host support policy, shader artifact formats, backend bootstrap steps, buffers, textures including shared texture usage intent, samplers, swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, backend-neutral swapchain resize, shaders, descriptor set layouts, descriptor sets, upload-buffer writes, copy/upload/readback commands with explicit texture footprints, CPU buffer readback, transient resource leases, presentation ownership and sequencing, single-pending-frame swapchain throttling, graphics pipelines with explicit vertex input layouts/attributes plus DepthStencilStateDesc depth-test/write/compare state, render passes with explicit clear colors plus optional RenderPassDepthAttachment and ClearDepthValue, descriptor set binding including sampler descriptors, vertex/index buffer binding, direct/indexed draw calls, resource transitions, fences, backend-neutral write/copy/read/fence/sampler lifecycle stats, shared texture export/failure stats, and a NullRhiDevice for backend-free validation of depth attachment ownership, usage, format, clear-depth range, extent, and pipeline color/depth compatibility plus null_mark_buffer_released/null_mark_texture_released/null_mark_sampler_released/null_mark_descriptor_set_released/null_mark_descriptor_set_layout_released/null_mark_pipeline_layout_released helpers for deterministic safe-point invalidation during host-owned teardown validation. Renderer/RHI Resource Foundation v1 adds a foundation-only value API in resource_lifetime.hpp through RhiResourceLifetimeRegistry, generation-checked first-party resource handles, explicit resource kinds, owner labels, debug names, live/deferred-release records, frame-indexed retirement, deterministic diagnostics, and enum-backed marker-style lifetime event rows. Upload/Staging Foundation v1 adds RhiUploadStagingPlan in upload_staging.hpp with staging allocation ids, buffer and texture copy records, deterministic copy-range diagnostics, submitted FenceValue tracking, and completed-fence retirement. Upload ring and staging pool execution v1 adds RhiUploadRing fence-tracked suballocations over one copy_source upload buffer, RhiStagingBufferPool freelists, validate_upload_gpu_batch, record_upload_gpu_batch, and mark_pending_allocations_submitted for IRhiCommandList buffer and buffer-to-texture copies with null-backend readback proof. These foundations do not migrate native backend destruction, implement vendor-specialized async GPU upload queues or streaming package integration, a GPU allocator, residency budgets, package streaming, GPU markers beyond the scoped API, editor resource management/capture tooling execution beyond reviewed request handoff, production renderer scheduling, or 2D/3D playable vertical-slice readiness. The D3D12 IRhiDevice bridge implements private DSV-backed public depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, readback-observable depth-test proof, sampled-depth proof, depth-aware postprocess proof, and directional shadow receiver proof; the Vulkan bridge implements private depth-aspect image views for sampled descriptors, depth|stencil D24S8 barrier aspect masks, required synchronization2 feature query/device enablement, depth_write synchronization2 barriers, dynamic-rendering depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, environment-gated depth-ordered readback proof, environment-gated visible sampled-depth readback proof, environment-gated postprocess-depth readback proof, and environment-gated directional shadow receiver readback proof while keeping Vulkan handles private.

modules.name: MK_rhi_d3d12
modules.purpose: Windows-only Direct3D 12 backend scaffold that verifies Windows SDK D3D12/DXGI compile integration, links official D3D12/DXGI libraries, probes DXGI factory plus hardware/WARP D3D12 support, validates minimal device, direct command queue, fence bootstrap, creates first committed upload/readback/default texture resources, and exposes a PIMPL DeviceContext for persistent D3D12 device/resource/command list/fence/swapchain/RTV/DSV/descriptor heap/root signature/shader/PSO ownership with CPU waits, GPU-side cross-queue waits, HWND presentation, swapchain resize, back-buffer transitions, standalone texture transitions, requested-color swapchain and texture render-target clears, requested-depth DSV clears, shader-visible descriptor heap binding, native CBV/SRV/UAV descriptor view writes, native sampler descriptor writes, graphics root signature binding, graphics descriptor table binding, sampler descriptor table splitting by heap kind, swapchain and texture render-target setup, private depth-stencil view setup, graphics PSO binding with optional input layouts and native depth-stencil state, vertex/index input-assembler view binding, upload-buffer writes, buffer and aligned buffer/texture copy recording, readback buffer mapping, shared committed texture creation for TextureUsage::shared, first-party shared texture export handles, and direct/indexed draw recording. It also exposes an initial IRhiDevice bridge for backend-neutral buffers, textures including depth24_stencil8 depth_stencil and depth_stencil|shader_resource textures, sampler handle validation, upload-buffer writes, descriptor set layout/allocation/update validation backed by separate CBV/SRV/UAV and sampler shader-visible descriptor arenas with native descriptor writes, shader bytecode, pipeline layouts, graphics pipelines with vertex input layouts/attributes and DepthStencilStateDesc mapping, buffer copies, aligned buffer/texture copies, CPU buffer readback, standalone texture transition commands with tracked before/after state validation, committed-resource-backed transient leases, surface-backed swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, backend-neutral swapchain resize, graphics-queue-only queued presentation, render-pass-complete-before-present sequencing, duplicate-present rejection, single-pending-frame throttling until release or present/submit, swapchain and texture render passes with optional private DSV depth attachments, descriptor set binding across CBV/SRV/UAV and sampler root tables, vertex/index buffer binding, direct/indexed draw recording, command submission, fence wait ownership, backend-neutral write/copy/read/fence/sampler stats, shared texture export/failure stats, non-black render-target clear readback proof, first-triangle texture readback proof, depth-tested overlapping-triangle texture readback proof, sampled-depth readback proof through private typeless DSV/SRV views, visible sampled-texture-plus-sampler readback proof, D3D12-visible RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling proof, D3D12-visible uploaded runtime mesh vertex/index buffer readback proof, D3D12-visible cooked runtime scene GPU palette indexed draw/readback proof, installed SDL3 packaged cooked-scene GPU binding proof with target-specific scene DXIL artifacts and strict scene-GPU smoke arguments, shared texture export proof, and swapchain lifecycle proof while native D3D12 handles remain backend-private and validate-gated by the public API boundary check.

modules.name: MK_rhi_vulkan
modules.purpose: SDK-independent Vulkan backend scaffold exposing mirakana::rhi::vulkan backend identity, host/runtime/device probing, private instance/device/command-pool owners, first-party buffer and texture owners, sampler owners, shader modules, descriptor set layout/pool/set update and binding, pipeline layout and graphics pipeline ownership with explicit vertex input layouts/attributes, Win32 surface/swapchain/image-view ownership, real surface capability/format/present-mode queries before swapchain creation, required dynamic rendering and synchronization2 feature query/device enablement, frame sync, acquire/present, dynamic rendering clear/draw recording, synchronization2 submission with shader-read texture barrier planning, depth|stencil D24S8 image-barrier aspect masks, command-list-local texture state commits, stale-submit rejection, readback buffers, SPIR-V artifact validation, and a mapping-plan-gated create_rhi_device IRhiDevice bridge for backend-neutral buffers, textures, sampler descriptors, upload-buffer writes, transient leases, shader modules, descriptor sets, descriptor binding that must match the bound pipeline layout, standalone texture transitions/copy/render-target-clear/render-target-draw commands, pipeline creation, vertex/index buffer binding, indexed draw recording, surface-backed swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, queued present, copy-only submit, fence stats/waits, readback-buffer reads, and native sampled-image/sampler descriptor writes through private runtime owners without including Vulkan headers or exposing native handles. SDL3/Vulkan game-window presentation is validated on a ready Windows host with precompiled SPIR-V artifacts; visible texture-sampling, depth-ordered draw, sampled-depth readback, postprocess-depth readback, and directional shadow receiver readback proofs remain environment-gated by local Vulkan runtime plus real SPIR-V artifacts.

modules.name: MK_rhi_metal
modules.purpose: SDK-independent Metal backend scaffold exposing mirakana::rhi::metal backend identity, host support, capability profile, probe plan, deterministic probe result helpers, non-empty metallib shader library artifact validation, runtime/default-device/command-queue/shader-library readiness planning, texture synchronization planning for render/blit/present encoder intent, platform availability diagnostics, and Apple-only native default-device, command-queue, command-buffer, render-encoder, texture-target, CAMetalLayer drawable acquisition, drawable present scheduling, command-buffer submission, and texture-readback ownership through PIMPL without including Metal or QuartzCore headers or exposing native handles. Apple Objective-C++ sources assign GameEngine.RHI.Metal.* GPU debugger labels via -[MTLObject setLabel:] on created Metal objects when linked.

modules.name: MK_scene
modules.purpose: Scene graph foundation for editor hierarchy and runtime scene loading. Currently provides rotation-aware transforms, parent/child relationships, camera/light/mesh/sprite renderer components, backend-neutral 2D playable scene validation through mirakana::validate_playable_2d_scene for primary orthographic camera, visible sprite, and visible mesh rejection diagnostics, renderer-neutral render packet extraction with visible meshes, visible sprites, world transforms, scene-subtree prefab creation, prefab instantiation, PrefabInstantiateDesc path metadata, ScenePrefabSourceLink / SceneNode::prefab_source source identity rows for instantiated prefab nodes, deterministic prefab text serialization, dependency-free prefab variant/override value types with diagnostics, composition into plain PrefabDefinition, deterministic GameEngine.PrefabVariant.v1 text serialization, and deterministic scene text serialization that preserves component data plus source-link rows. Scene/Component/Prefab Schema v2 is implemented as a contract-only public MK_scene data spine through stable AuthoringId values, schema-driven SceneComponentDocumentV2 rows, deterministic validation diagnostics, GameEngine.Scene.v2 serialization/deserialization, GameEngine.Prefab.v2 validation/serialization/deserialization, and stable prefab override paths for node name/parent and component property overrides. Runtime package migration, editor productization, nested prefab propagation/merge resolution UX, production 2D atlas/tilemap/native GPU output, and ready 3D playable vertical slices remain follow-up work.

and Stable Directional Light-Space Policy v0 plan data, including SceneMaterialPalette base-color resolution from MaterialDefinition and composed MaterialInstanceDefinition overrides, directional shadow-casting light and counts visible meshes as v0 casters/receivers, build_scene_directional_shadow_light_space_plan adaptation that derives mesh-center bounds plus selected directional light direction for DirectionalShadowLightSpacePlan without native handles, package-level load_runtime_scene_render_data that delegates cooked scene instantiation and reference validation to MK_runtime_scene before building a SceneMaterialPalette, instantiate_runtime_scene_render_data for producing a non-throwing Scene plus SceneRenderPacket runtime render instance with render-packet build failures reported as diagnostics, sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_float_clip for applying cooked scalar float animation clips plus AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance scene data through MK_runtime_scene binding resolution and rebuilding the render packet after successful mutation, sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d for sampling AnimationJointTrack3dDesc rows into AnimationPose3d, applying named-joint pose rows to runtime scene node transforms through MK_runtime_scene, and rebuilding the render packet after successful mutation, sample_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_animation_float_clip for sampling cooked scalar morph-weight clips against RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload rows and returning deterministic CPU-morphed positions for generated package smoke telemetry, preservation of scene mesh/material asset ids on MeshCommand, deterministic 3D mesh position/scale/z-rotation extraction, 2D sprite position/scale/rotation/tint extraction, camera view/projection matrix generation, and renderer-neutral light command extraction from scene world matrices, without making MK_scene depend on MK_renderer, MK_runtime, or RHI backends.

graphics.name: null
graphics.notes: Use NullRenderer and NullRhiDevice for deterministic generated-game and engine tests without GPU or window dependencies, including backend-neutral runtime texture upload, mesh vertex/index buffer upload, and material descriptor binding coverage.

graphics.name: d3d12
graphics.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/build-gui.ps1
graphics.notes: Visible clear/draw/readback, swapchain lifecycle, shared texture export, editor viewport display, SDL3 desktop game-window RhiFrameRenderer creation from host-supplied precompiled shader bytecode, installed packaged cooked-scene GPU binding validation with target-specific scene DXIL artifacts, installed depth-aware Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 validation with target-specific postprocess DXIL artifacts and postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, installed package-visible Directional Shadow Filtering v0 validation with target-specific shadow receiver/shadow DXIL artifacts, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, and framegraph_passes=3, plus Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 validation with --require-renderer-quality-gates, renderer_quality_status=ready, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1 are implemented through backend-private Direct3D 12 ownership.

graphics.name: vulkan
graphics.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1; out\build\desktop-runtime\games\Debug\sample_desktop_runtime_shell.exe --smoke --video-driver windows --require-vulkan-shaders --require-vulkan-renderer; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V draw/readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V texture-sampling proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV, and MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V sampled-depth readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, and MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V postprocess-depth readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV, and MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V shadow receiver readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV for real SPIR-V compute dispatch readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV for Runtime RHI compute morph POSITION readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV for Runtime RHI compute morph renderer-consumption proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV for Runtime RHI compute morph NORMAL/TANGENT readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV for cooked scene GPU palette proof
graphics.notes: Runtime owners and the IRhiDevice bridge cover backend-neutral resource/shader/descriptor/pipeline/swapchain creation, standalone texture draw/readback, swapchain clear/present, command submission, fence stats, readback-domain buffer reads, private depth image-view/layout/barrier/dynamic-rendering depth attachments, required synchronization2 feature enablement, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, and actual surface capability/format/present-mode queries before native swapchain creation. SDL3/Vulkan game-window RhiFrameRenderer creation, host-owned cooked-scene GPU binding injection, depth-aware Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 package proof from precompiled SPIR-V artifacts with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, package-visible Directional Shadow Filtering v0 proof with target-specific shadow receiver/shadow SPIR-V artifacts, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, and framegraph_passes=3, plus Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 validation with --require-renderer-quality-gates, renderer_quality_status=ready, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1 is validated on this Windows host when Vulkan runtime, surface support, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and spirv-val are available; Linux and generated-game Vulkan availability remain host/toolchain gated. The visible draw/readback, texture-sampling, runtime material sampling, cooked-scene palette, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, postprocess-depth readback, and shadow receiver readback proofs are environment-gated by local Vulkan runtime plus real SPIR-V artifacts.

platform.name: mobile-contracts
platform.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1
platform.notes: SDK-independent mobile lifecycle, touch, orientation, safe-area, storage, and permission contracts are implemented; Android GameActivity and Apple package templates adapt into these contracts without exposing native handles.

importerCapabilities.runtimePolicy: Game runtime code consumes cooked artifacts and must not parse external source formats directly.

packagingTargets.name: source-tree-default
packagingTargets.notes: Builds and tests the engine and sample games from the source tree.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-gui
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the optional SDL3, Dear ImGui, editor, and SDL3 audio adapter lane.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-game-runtime
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the optional editor-independent SDL3 desktop game runtime samples through the SDL3-only desktop-runtime preset, plus SDL3 platform/audio tests, runtime host tests including DiagnosticsRecorder-backed frame profiles and renderer counter capture, SDL3 event-pump tests, reusable SdlDesktopGameHost tests, SDL3 presentation fallback/report tests including scene GPU binding unavailable, postprocess request validation, directional shadow request/filter metadata validation, Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 evaluation, and Vulkan request diagnostics, manifest-linked generated registered-game package metadata including manifest-derived package file source/install paths and target-specific shader artifact paths, finite sample_desktop_runtime_shell --smoke, DXC-available build-output DXIL artifact smoke paths, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen-available build-output SPIR-V artifact smoke paths, the non-shell sample_desktop_runtime_game --smoke path, the config-only sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package scaffold proof generated by tools/new-game.ps1 -Template DesktopRuntimePackage, the sample_generated_desktop_runtime_cooked_scene_package scaffold proof generated by tools/new-game.ps1 -Template DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage with --require-scene-package, and the DXC-available sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package_shader_artifacts_smoke source-tree proof generated by tools/new-game.ps1 -Template DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage. The reusable host gives games selecting desktop-game-runtime a first-party SDL3 windowed host path without copying sample host assembly; the samples use SdlDesktopGameHost and SdlDesktopPresentation for host-owned renderer selection with D3D12/Vulkan-ready surface probing, optional build-output D3D12 DXIL or Vulkan SPIR-V shader bytecode RhiFrameRenderer creation, host-owned packaged D3D12/Vulkan scene GPU binding, host-owned Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 setup including optional depth-input fallback/report diagnostics, host-owned package-visible fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF directional shadow filtering setup with first-party fallback/report diagnostics, package-visible renderer quality summary fields such as renderer_quality_status and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok, backend-neutral presentation report fields, and NullRenderer fallback diagnostics; DesktopGameRunner can drive swapchain-backed RhiFrameRenderer resize and optional observability capture through public IWindow/IRenderer/MK_core contracts.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-runtime-release
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the SDL3-only desktop-runtime-release preset, cleans the desktop runtime install prefix, installs a selected registered desktop runtime game executable plus selected package files derived from target metadata, selected D3D12 DXIL and explicitly requested Vulkan SPIR-V shader artifacts when declared, SDL3.dll on Windows, share/Mirakanai/desktop-runtime-games.json package metadata with selectedPackageTarget, gameManifest paths, packageFiles, d3d12ShaderArtifacts, and vulkanShaderArtifacts, validates the installed SDK consumer, runs the installed selected game smoke from metadata, verifies the selected installed game manifest, package files, required shader artifacts, and presentation/postprocess/directional-shadow/renderer-quality status fields, and creates a CPack ZIP without requiring Dear ImGui or MK_editor. pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 defaults to sample_desktop_runtime_shell with metadata-driven --smoke --require-d3d12-shaders --require-vulkan-shaders; tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package validates the generated config-only package scaffold with metadata-driven --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package.config and no shader/cooked-scene claims; tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_desktop_runtime_game validates the non-shell packaged scene proof with metadata-driven --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_desktop_runtime_game.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_desktop_runtime_game.geindex --require-d3d12-scene-shaders --video-driver windows --require-d3d12-renderer --require-scene-gpu-bindings --require-postprocess --require-postprocess-depth-input --require-directional-shadow --require-directional-shadow-filtering --require-renderer-quality-gates, including cooked scene package load, renderer-submit counters, target-specific scene/postprocess/shadow DXIL artifacts, host-owned D3D12 scene GPU binding counters, Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 status with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, Directional Shadow Filtering v0 status with directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, and framegraph_passes=3, and Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 status with renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_ready=1, renderer_quality_diagnostics=0, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_passes=3, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1. The same selected target can validate target-specific scene/postprocess/shadow SPIR-V artifacts and host-owned Vulkan scene GPU binding plus depth-aware postprocess/directional shadow filtering with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1, and -RequireVulkanShaders plus explicit Vulkan smoke args on a ready Windows/Vulkan host.

packagingTargets.name: asset-importers
packagingTargets.notes: Builds MK_tools with optional libspng, fastgltf, and miniaudio importer adapters and runs importer-capable tests.

packagingTargets.name: windows-cpack-zip
packagingTargets.notes: Builds Release, runs Release tests, installs the SDK layout, validates examples/installed_consumer against the installed mirakana:: package, and creates a CPack ZIP with package metadata.

packagingTargets.name: android-gameactivity
packagingTargets.notes: Android Gradle Plugin 9.1.0, Gradle 9.3.1, JDK 17, Android SDK Platform 36.1, Build Tools 36.0.0, Android SDK CMake 4.1.2, NDK CMake, Prefab, AndroidX AppCompat 1.7.1, AndroidX Core 1.18.0, GameActivity 3.0.4, NDK Vulkan loader linkage, private ANativeWindow/VK_KHR_android_surface creation bridge, private low-latency AAudio float32 output adapter, generated asset packaging, host-validated Debug/Release APK output, Release signing verification with non-repository upload keys, upload certificate export, deterministic ANDROID_AVD_HOME normalization, AVD readiness diagnostics, and emulator install/launch smoke coverage exist; generated game manifests are validated and packaged before package build attempts, and pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1 reports signing/toolchain/AVD/device blockers explicitly.

packagingTargets.name: ios-metal
packagingTargets.notes: Apple CMake/Xcode iOS bundle template exists with UIKit/Metal framework linkage, Metal-backed root view setup, sandbox save/cache/shared storage root mapping, game manifest resource packaging, bundle identifier inputs, simulator/device SDK selection, simulator signing suppression, Xcode signing inputs, iOS Simulator install/launch smoke scripting through pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/smoke-ios-package.ps1, and explicit full-Xcode/iOS-SDK/iOS-runtime blockers from pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1; actual host-validated simulator/device results and backend-neutral Metal presentation remain Apple-host gated.

validationRecipes.name: default
validationRecipes.purpose: Run the repository default correctness gate before reporting completion.

validationRecipes.name: agent-contract
validationRecipes.command: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-ai-integration.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-json-contracts.ps1
validationRecipes.purpose: Validate AI instructions, manifests, schemas, and generated context.

validationRecipes.name: public-api-boundary
validationRecipes.purpose: Check public headers and backend interop boundaries after public API or handle-boundary changes.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-gui
validationRecipes.purpose: Build and test optional SDL3, Dear ImGui, editor, and SDL3 audio adapter code.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-game-runtime
validationRecipes.purpose: Build and test optional SDL3 desktop game runtime host code through the SDL3-only desktop-runtime preset, including manifest-linked generated registered-game metadata with package file path validation, SDL3 platform/audio tests, runtime host tests, the SDL event pump, reusable SdlDesktopGameHost tests, SDL presentation fallback/required-bytecode tests, finite sample_desktop_runtime_shell smoke execution, DXC-available build-output DXIL shader artifact smoke, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen-available build-output SPIR-V shader artifact smoke, non-shell sample_desktop_runtime_game smoke execution, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package config-only scaffold smoke execution, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_cooked_scene_package cooked-scene scaffold smoke execution, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package material/shader scaffold smoke execution, and DXC-available sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package_shader_artifacts_smoke source-tree shader-artifact execution. Real-window SDL3/D3D12 or SDL3/Vulkan renderer smokes remain explicit local commands because they open a native desktop window.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-release-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the optional Release desktop runtime package, install the selected registered runtime game executable, SDL3 runtime DLL, selected package files, generated desktop-runtime game package metadata with gameManifest and packageFiles paths derived from manifest-driven or literal registration, optional generated D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V shader artifacts for the current sample shell shader-artifact package path, validate installed SDK metadata, validate a clean installed consumer, validate the installed selected game manifest, package files, shader artifacts, and smoke from metadata, including selected-target cooked package load and renderer-submit proofs when the game metadata requires them, and create the CPack package artifact.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-shell-vulkan-window-smoke
validationRecipes.purpose: On a Windows host with Vulkan runtime, surface support, and generated SPIR-V artifacts, validate the SDL3/Vulkan game-window RhiFrameRenderer path without exposing Vulkan or SDL handles to gameplay.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-generated-scaffold-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected generated desktop runtime package scaffold lane and validate installed sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package with manifest-derived runtime config package files, metadata-selected --require-config smoke args, and deterministic NullRenderer fallback without D3D12/Vulkan shader artifacts or cooked-scene claims.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-generated-cooked-scene-scaffold-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected generated desktop runtime cooked-scene package scaffold lane and validate installed sample_generated_desktop_runtime_cooked_scene_package with manifest-derived config, .geindex, first-party cooked texture/mesh/material/scene files, metadata-selected --require-config plus --require-scene-package smoke args, runtime package loading, renderer-neutral scene submission counters, and deterministic NullRenderer fallback without D3D12/Vulkan shader artifacts or scene GPU binding claims.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-2d-package-proof
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected 2D desktop runtime package proof and validate installed sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package with manifest-derived config, .geindex, first-party cooked texture/material/audio/scene files, metadata-selected --require-config plus --require-scene-package smoke args, cooked package loading, playable 2D scene validation, sprite/HUD/audio counters, packaged D3D12 DXIL shader artifacts, host-gated D3D12 package window smoke, and --require-native-2d-sprites native 2D sprite batch execution counters such as native_2d_sprite_batches_executed and native_2d_sprite_batch_texture_binds without runtime image decoding and production atlas/editor/runtime integration beyond the reviewed source-image decode and atlas-packing tools, public native/RHI handles, Metal, broad production sprite batching readiness, or broad renderer-quality claims.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-2d-vulkan-window-package
validationRecipes.purpose: On a ready Windows/Vulkan host with DXC SPIR-V CodeGen and spirv-val, build the selected 2D desktop runtime package proof and validate installed sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package with packaged SPIR-V shader artifacts, --require-vulkan-renderer smoke args, presentation_selected=vulkan, cooked 2D scene validation, sprite/HUD/audio counters, and no public native/RHI handle exposure. This remains a strict host/toolchain-gated presentation smoke, not broad production sprite batching readiness, Metal, or broad renderer-quality readiness.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-generated-material-shader-scaffold-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected generated desktop runtime material/shader scaffold lane and validate installed sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package with manifest-derived config and cooked scene package files, source material/HLSL authoring inputs kept out of runtimePackageFiles, target-specific D3D12 scene/postprocess DXIL artifacts, host-owned D3D12 scene GPU bindings, and Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 status through backend-neutral presentation report fields. The source-tree counterpart is covered by the desktop-game-runtime recipe through the DXC-available sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package_shader_artifacts_smoke CTest.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-generated-material-shader-scaffold-package-vulkan-strict
validationRecipes.purpose: On a ready Windows/Vulkan host with DXC SPIR-V CodeGen and spirv-val, build and validate installed sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package with packaged SPIR-V scene/postprocess artifacts, presentation_selected=vulkan, --require-vulkan-renderer, --require-scene-gpu-bindings, and --require-postprocess, matching the per-game desktop-runtime-release-target-vulkan-toolchain-gated recipe row. This is strict toolchain/package evidence for the material/shader scaffold lane aligned to editor material-preview Vulkan readiness workstreams; it does not claim full mirakana_editor material-preview display parity or cross-backend visual equivalence.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-sample-game-scene-gpu-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected non-shell desktop runtime package lane and validate installed sample_desktop_runtime_game with packaged config, cooked scene package loading, target-specific scene/postprocess/shadow/UI-overlay DXIL artifacts by default, metadata-selected --require-d3d12-renderer smoke args, --require-scene-gpu-bindings, --require-postprocess-depth-input, --require-directional-shadow, --require-directional-shadow-filtering, --require-renderer-quality-gates, --require-native-ui-overlay, --require-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas, Lit Material v0 position+normal+uv/base-color/direct-light shader behavior, backend-neutral D3D12 scene GPU binding counters, postprocess_status=ready, postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_ready=1, renderer_quality_diagnostics=0, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_passes=3, renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1, ui_overlay_status=ready, ui_overlay_ready=1, positive ui_overlay_sprites_submitted/ui_overlay_draws, ui_texture_overlay_status=ready, ui_texture_overlay_atlas_ready=1, positive ui_texture_overlay_sprites_submitted/ui_texture_overlay_texture_binds/ui_texture_overlay_draws, and framegraph_passes=3.

validationRecipes.name: installed-d3d12-3d-shadow-morph-composition-smoke
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the committed generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage proof and validate the selected D3D12 graphics morph + directional shadow receiver package smoke with shifted shadow receiver artifacts, positive scene_gpu_morph_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_morph_mesh_resolved, renderer_gpu_morph_draws, renderer_morph_descriptor_binds, directional_shadow_ready=1, fixed_pcf_3x3 filtering, and framegraph_passes=3 without claiming compute morph + shadow composition, morph-deformed shadow-caster silhouettes, Vulkan/Metal parity, public native/RHI handles, or broad renderer quality.

validationRecipes.name: installed-d3d12-3d-native-ui-overlay-smoke
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the committed generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage proof and validate the selected D3D12 native UI overlay HUD box package smoke with generated UI overlay DXIL artifacts, hud_boxes=2, ui_overlay_requested=1, ui_overlay_status=ready, ui_overlay_ready=1, ui_overlay_sprites_submitted=2, and ui_overlay_draws=2 without claiming textured image-sprite atlas behavior, production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, Metal overlay readiness, public native/RHI handles, or broad generated 3D production readiness.

validationRecipes.purpose: Build the committed generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage proof and validate the selected D3D12 visible production-style aggregate with visible_3d_status=ready, visible_3d_ready=1, visible_3d_diagnostics=0, visible_3d_presented_frames=2, D3D12 selection, no NullRenderer fallback, scene GPU, postprocess, renderer-quality, playable_3d, gameplay systems, and native UI overlay readiness while avoiding Vulkan/Metal parity, native/RHI handle exposure, broad renderer quality, or broad generated 3D production readiness claims.

validationRecipes.name: installed-d3d12-3d-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas-smoke

validationRecipes.purpose: Build the committed generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage proof and validate the selected D3D12 cooked UI atlas text-glyph package smoke with generated UI overlay DXIL artifacts, runtime/assets/3d/hud_text.uiatlas, hud_text_glyphs=2, text_glyphs_available=2, text_glyphs_resolved=2, text_glyphs_missing=0, text_glyph_sprites_submitted=2, ui_atlas_metadata_status=ready, ui_atlas_metadata_pages=1, ui_atlas_metadata_bindings=0, ui_atlas_metadata_glyphs=1, ui_texture_overlay_status=ready, ui_texture_overlay_atlas_ready=1, ui_texture_overlay_sprites_submitted=2, ui_texture_overlay_texture_binds=2, and ui_texture_overlay_draws=2 without claiming production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, source image atlas packing, Metal overlay readiness, public native/RHI handles, or broad generated 3D production readiness.

validationRecipes.purpose: On a ready Windows/Vulkan host with DXC SPIR-V CodeGen and spirv-val, build the selected non-shell desktop runtime package lane and validate installed sample_desktop_runtime_game with target-specific scene/postprocess/shadow SPIR-V artifacts, --require-vulkan-renderer smoke args, --require-scene-gpu-bindings, --require-postprocess, --require-postprocess-depth-input, --require-directional-shadow, --require-directional-shadow-filtering, --require-renderer-quality-gates, Lit Material v0 position+normal+uv/base-color/direct-light shader behavior, presentation_selected=vulkan, postprocess_status=ready, postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1, framegraph_passes=3, and backend-neutral Vulkan scene GPU binding counters.

validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected non-shell desktop runtime package lane and validate installed sample_desktop_runtime_game on the primary D3D12 Windows path with renderer-owned native GPU runtime UI overlay output for MK_ui_renderer box/image-placeholder sprite submissions, target-specific UI overlay DXIL artifacts, ui_overlay_requested=1, ui_overlay_status=ready, ui_overlay_ready=1, and positive ui_overlay_sprites_submitted/ui_overlay_draws while keeping production text/font/image/atlas/accessibility unsupported.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-sample-game-vulkan-native-ui-overlay-package
validationRecipes.purpose: On a ready Windows/Vulkan host with DXC SPIR-V CodeGen and spirv-val, validate the same renderer-owned native GPU runtime UI overlay proof through strict Vulkan package smoke with target-specific UI overlay SPIR-V artifacts, presentation_selected=vulkan, ui_overlay_requested=1, ui_overlay_status=ready, ui_overlay_ready=1, and positive ui_overlay_sprites_submitted/ui_overlay_draws.

validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected non-shell desktop runtime package lane and validate installed sample_desktop_runtime_game on the primary D3D12 Windows path with a renderer-owned sampled UI sprite bound from cooked texture metadata through UiRendererImageBinding atlas_page/uv_rect, --require-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas, ui_texture_overlay_requested=1, ui_texture_overlay_status=ready, ui_texture_overlay_atlas_ready=1, and positive ui_texture_overlay_sprites_submitted/ui_texture_overlay_texture_binds/ui_texture_overlay_draws while runtime image decoding and production atlas/editor/runtime integration beyond the reviewed source-image decode and atlas-packing tools, text/font/accessibility, Metal, public native handles, and broad UI renderer quality remain unsupported.

validationRecipes.purpose: On a ready Windows/Vulkan host with DXC SPIR-V CodeGen and spirv-val, validate the same cooked texture/atlas-backed UI sprite proof through strict Vulkan package smoke with target-specific UI overlay SPIR-V artifacts, presentation_selected=vulkan, ui_texture_overlay_requested=1, ui_texture_overlay_status=ready, ui_texture_overlay_atlas_ready=1, and positive ui_texture_overlay_sprites_submitted/ui_texture_overlay_texture_binds/ui_texture_overlay_draws.
