validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected sample_desktop_runtime_game desktop runtime package and validate that first-party cooked UI atlas package metadata is installed, loaded through MK_runtime, converted into UiRendererImagePalette bindings by MK_ui_renderer, and reported with ui_atlas_metadata_status=ready, ui_atlas_metadata_pages=1, ui_atlas_metadata_bindings=1, plus the existing ready textured UI overlay fields.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-sample-game-vulkan-ui-atlas-metadata-package
validationRecipes.purpose: On a ready Windows/Vulkan host with DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, spirv-val, and Vulkan runtime readiness, validate the same UI atlas package metadata proof through strict Vulkan package smoke with presentation_selected=vulkan, framegraph_passes=3, ui_atlas_metadata_status=ready, ui_atlas_metadata_pages=1, ui_atlas_metadata_bindings=1, and ready textured UI overlay counters.

validationRecipes.name: shader-toolchain
validationRecipes.purpose: Report D3D12 DXIL, Vulkan SPIR-V, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, spirv-val, Metal, and metallib readiness, using MK_SHADER_TOOLCHAIN_ROOT, User/Machine PATH, Vulkan SDK variables, vcpkg tool roots, and Windows SDK fallback discovery.

validationRecipes.name: dev-windows-editor-game-module-driver-load-tests
validationRecipes.purpose: On Windows with MSVC dev preset tooling, configure dev, build MK_editor_game_module_driver_probe and MK_editor_game_module_driver_load_tests, and run CTest MK_editor_game_module_driver_load_tests for same-engine-build DLL load, factory resolution, isolated session tick, stopped-state unload plus second load, and paired DynamicLibrary refcount ordering without mirakana_editor execution, active-session hot reload, or stable third-party ABI claims.

validationRecipes.name: dependency-policy
validationRecipes.purpose: Verify vcpkg, dependency docs, and third-party notice policy.

validationRecipes.name: asset-importers
validationRecipes.purpose: Validate optional PNG, glTF, and common audio source importer adapters behind MK_tools.

validationRecipes.name: release-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build Release, run Release tests, validate installed SDK metadata, validate an installed SDK consumer project, create the CPack package artifact, validate the current ZIP SHA-256 sidecar, and inspect archived SDK metadata/tool/doc/license entries.

validationRecipes.name: installed-sdk
validationRecipes.purpose: Validate installed SDK metadata payloads, then build examples/installed_consumer against an installed GameEngine package without source-tree include paths.

validationRecipes.name: mobile-packaging
validationRecipes.purpose: Validate mobile packaging templates and report Android SDK/NDK/JDK/Gradle/AVD/device or Apple full-Xcode/iOS-SDK/iOS-runtime blockers explicitly without faking package readiness.

validationRecipes.name: ios-simulator-smoke
validationRecipes.purpose: On macOS/Xcode hosts, build the iOS Simulator bundle, install it with xcrun simctl, verify the app container, launch it, and clean up the simulator state.

safePointPackageReplacementLoops.summary: Host-owned safe-point package replacement and unload execution for preloaded runtime packages. Replacement rebuilds a RuntimeResourceCatalogV2 from the pending package, commits the RuntimeAssetPackageStore and catalog together only at a safe point, and rolls back invalid pending packages before active swap. Unload clears the active package and commits an empty catalog only at a safe point, discarding pending replacement state when an active package exists. Both paths report deterministic generation and stale-handle invalidation counters without executing async streaming, eviction, renderer/RHI teardown, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, or renderer quality claims.

orderedSteps.notes: Only a successfully loaded RuntimeAssetPackage may enter pending state; failed package loads leave the active package untouched.

Duplicate or invalid package records keep the existing active package/catalog live and clear the pending package.

Clear the active package and replace the catalog with an empty generation only when an active package exists; pending replacements are discarded on successful unload and preserved when no active package exists.
Renderer/RHI teardown and deferred release remain host-owned follow-up work; gameplay and manifests still do not receive IRhiDevice, native handles, descriptor handles, or GPU residency objects.

requiredBeforeReadyClaim: Linux coverage threshold policy with stable CI evidence
sanitizer CTest lane green with documented matrix
build and package CI matrix hardening
unsupportedProductionGaps.notes: Strict clang-tidy compile database synthesis for the default Windows Visual Studio dev preset is ready (see host gate tidy-compile-database). Tidy Targeted File Lane v1 adds wrapper-owned targeted changed-file clang-tidy through tools/check-tidy.ps1 -Files while preserving the default full-repository behavior and validate-scoped -MaxFiles 1 smoke. Phase 1 slices completed: full-repository tidy cleanup, cmake install/export + module audit, sanitizer CTest lane + CI matrix documentation, Linux line-coverage minimum via tools/coverage-thresholds.json and check-coverage.ps1 -Strict on CI, CI Matrix Contract Check v1 via tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1 / pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1 as a default validation contract over reviewed Windows/Linux/sanitizer/macOS/iOS workflow lanes (including the ci-matrix-check contract in validate workflow), Windows package artifacts, Linux coverage artifacts, test-log artifacts, least-privilege workflow token permissions, stale pull-request run cancellation, and host-gated iOS workflow evidence, C++23 Release Package Artifact CI Evidence v1 via tools/evaluate-cpp23.ps1 -Release running Assert-ReleasePackageArtifacts after CPack plus .zip.sha256 workflow upload for the existing Windows C++23 release lane, and Full Repository Static Analysis CI Contract v1 via the validate.yml static-analysis job running tools/check-tidy.ps1 -Strict -Jobs 0 on ubuntu-latest with automatic CPU-count parallelism plus static-analysis and static-analysis-tidy-logs artifacts. Remaining gap items: broader build/package CI execution evidence beyond the static workflow contracts and Windows C++23 release package artifact lane, broader static analyzer profile expansion beyond the current .clang-tidy contract and ci-matrix-check scope; remaining broader analyzer limits are explicit here until full-repository quality gates are complete.

hostGates.notes: D3D12 is the primary currently verified Windows visible/package lane, but gameplay APIs still must not expose D3D12 or RHI handles.
Vulkan package/visible claims require explicit strict smoke commands and local runtime/toolchain readiness. Do not generalize Windows proof to other hosts.
Metal visible presentation and Apple package readiness remain Apple-host gated and must not be marked ready from Windows validation.

hosts: configured signing and AVD/device when required
hostGates.notes: Android Debug/Release/signing/emulator lanes depend on mobile-check output and selected game package commands.

hosts: WIN32 CMake targets MK_editor_game_module_driver_probe and MK_editor_game_module_driver_load_tests
hostGates.notes: Windows-only editor game module driver reload transaction CTest lane requires local MSVC dev preset build outputs for the probe shared library and load-test executable. Does not launch mirakana_editor or claim active-session hot reload or stable third-party ABI.

hosts: default Windows Visual Studio dev preset through CMake File API codemodel
Makefile or Ninja generators with native compile_commands.json
hostGates.notes: Strict clang-tidy compile database availability is ready for the default dev validation path. tools/check-tidy.ps1 verifies .clang-tidy, requests CMake File API codemodel data and configures the preset when native compile_commands.json is unavailable, synthesizes clang-tidy arguments rows for Visual Studio generator builds, supports -Files for targeted changed-file clang-tidy on compile-database source files, and still reports missing CMake or clang-tidy as local tool blockers.

documentationPolicy.copyingPolicy: Use documentation for guidance; do not copy external code unless the license is explicit and recorded.

sourcePolicy.vcpkgConfigurePolicy: CMake configure must not install, restore, or download vcpkg packages; vcpkg-enabled presets use VCPKG_MANIFEST_INSTALL=OFF and VCPKG_INSTALLED_DIR=${sourceDir}/vcpkg_installed.

gameCodeGuidance.entryPoint: Implement mirakana::GameApp and run it through a runner.
gameCodeGuidance.currentEditorPrefabVariantFileDialogs: Editor Prefab Variant Native Dialog v1 is the first non-Profiler native document dialog slice. MK_editor_core exposes EditorPrefabVariantFileDialogModel, make_prefab_variant_open_dialog_request, make_prefab_variant_save_dialog_request, make_prefab_variant_open_dialog_model, make_prefab_variant_save_dialog_model, and make_prefab_variant_file_dialog_ui_model to build/review .prefabvariant open/save FileDialogRequest/FileDialogResult rows under retained prefab_variant_file_dialog.open and prefab_variant_file_dialog.save ids without SDL3, Dear ImGui, native handles, filesystem IO, or project-store path conversion in editor core. The visible MK_editor Prefab Variant Authoring panel exposes Browse Load Variant and Browse Save Variant through mirakana::IFileDialogService / mirakana::SdlFileDialogService, polls asynchronous results, converts accepted in-project .prefabvariant selections back to safe project-relative paths, and then reuses existing load_prefab_variant_authoring_document / save_prefab_variant_authoring_document paths. Broader editor native save/open dialogs outside Profiler, Scene, and Prefab Variant, arbitrary project browsing, package scripts, validation recipes, dynamic game-module/runtime-host Play-In-Editor execution, renderer/RHI uploads, native handles, package streaming, and broad editor productization remain unsupported.

MK_editor_core exposes EditorSceneFileDialogModel, EditorSceneFileDialogMode, make_scene_open_dialog_request, make_scene_save_dialog_request, make_scene_open_dialog_model, make_scene_save_dialog_model, make_scene_file_dialog_ui_model, and SceneAuthoringDocument::set_scene_path to build/review .scene open/save FileDialogRequest/FileDialogResult rows under retained scene_file_dialog.open and scene_file_dialog.save ids without SDL3, Dear ImGui, native handles, filesystem IO, or project-store path conversion in editor core. The visible MK_editor File menu and Scene panel expose Open Scene..., Save Scene As..., Browse Open Scene, and Browse Save Scene As through mirakana::IFileDialogService / mirakana::SdlFileDialogService, poll asynchronous results, convert accepted in-project .scene selections back to safe project-relative paths, update the current SceneAuthoringDocument path, and then reuse existing load_scene_authoring_document / save_scene_authoring_document paths. Broader editor native save/open dialogs outside Profiler, Scene, and Prefab Variant, arbitrary project browsing, package scripts, validation recipes, dynamic game-module/runtime-host Play-In-Editor execution, renderer/RHI uploads, native handles, package streaming, and broad editor productization remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.currentEditorPrefabInstanceSourceLinks: Editor Prefab Instance Source-Link Review v1 is implemented through MK_scene ScenePrefabSourceLink, PrefabInstantiateDesc, SceneNode::prefab_source, deterministic source-link scene text IO, MK_editor_core ScenePrefabInstanceSourceLinkModel, make_scene_prefab_instance_source_link_model, make_scene_prefab_instance_source_link_ui_model, and retained scene_prefab_source_links rows. Editor Scene Prefab Instance Refresh Review v1, Editor Prefab Instance Local Child Refresh Resolution v1, Editor Prefab Instance Stale Node Refresh Resolution v1, and Editor Nested Prefab Refresh Resolution v1 are implemented through MK_editor_core ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshPlan, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshRow, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshResult, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshPolicy, plan_scene_prefab_instance_refresh, plan_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_batch, apply_scene_prefab_instance_refresh, apply_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_batch, make_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_action, make_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_batch_action, make_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_ui_model, make_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_batch_ui_model, scene_prefab_instance_refresh_batch, keep_local_child rows, keep_local_child_count, kept_local_child_count, keep_stale_source_node_as_local rows, keep_stale_source_node_count, kept_stale_source_node_count, keep_stale_source_nodes_as_local, unsupported_stale_source_subtree blockers, keep_nested_prefab_instance rows, unsupported_nested_prefab_instance blockers, keep_nested_prefab_instance_count, kept_nested_prefab_instance_count, keep_nested_prefab_instances, and retained scene_prefab_instance_refresh rows. The visible MK_editor Scene panel passes saved prefab and composed prefab-variant paths into path-aware make_scene_authoring_instantiate_prefab_action calls, renders linked/stale source-link diagnostics, and exposes Keep Local Children, Keep Stale Source Nodes, Keep Nested Prefab Instances, and Refresh Prefab Instance only for selected linked roots with safe prefab paths. The refresh review preserves existing scene node state for retained unique source-node names, adds new refreshed source nodes, removes reviewed stale source nodes by default, can keep reviewed stale source-node subtrees as local author-owned nodes only when keep_stale_source_nodes_as_local is explicitly enabled, can preserve reviewed nested linked prefab subtrees only when keep_nested_prefab_instances is explicitly enabled, blocks non-root links and ambiguous source names, blocks unsupported local children and unsafe stale subtrees by default, preserves local child subtrees only when keep_local_children is explicitly enabled, and applies only through an explicit undoable action. gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dMorphNormalTangentPackag: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds ship cooked/source morph_mesh_cpu bind NORMAL/TANGENT streams plus target NORMAL/TANGENT delta streams, align generated scene vertex input to kRuntimeSceneTangentSpaceStrideBytes, declare tangent semantics, and generate vs_morph shader source that reads morph POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT delta buffers through descriptor bindings 0/2/3 with weights at binding 1. gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphPackageSmoke: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds can pass packaged compute morph shader bytecode through SdlDesktopPresentationD3D12SceneRendererDesc::compute_morph_vertex_shader and compute_morph_shader or SdlDesktopPresentationVulkanSceneRendererDesc::compute_morph_vertex_shader and compute_morph_shader, select explicit compute_morph_mesh_bindings rows, dispatch host-owned create_runtime_morph_mesh_compute_binding work against cooked POSITION data, render the compute-written POSITION output through make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding, and require scene_gpu_compute_morph_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_compute_morph_dispatches, scene_gpu_compute_morph_queue_waits, scene_gpu_compute_morph_mesh_resolved, and scene_gpu_compute_morph_draws in the D3D12 package smoke and toolchain-gated Vulkan package smoke. When --require-shadow-morph-composition is selected, desktopRuntime3dShadowMorphCompositionPackageSmoke covers the selected graphics morph + directional shadow receiver composition counters. This is generated 3D renderer quality smoke only; GPU timestamps, backend-native stats, native handles, Metal readiness, broad directional shadow production quality, morph-deformed shadow-caster silhouettes, broad shadow+morph composition, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.

Runtime RHI Compute Morph Async Overlap Evidence D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiAsyncOverlapReadinessDiagnostics and diagnose_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness, which deterministically reports not_proven_serial_dependency for the current same-frame graphics queue wait without exposing native handles, timestamp resources, or package-visible performance claims. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Output Ring D3D12 v1 adds RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBindingOptions::output_slot_count, RuntimeMorphMeshComputeOutputSlot, slot-indexed make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding, and D3D12 descriptor-range proof that slot 1 can receive compute-written POSITION output through binding 3 without aliasing slot 0. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Scheduling D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiPipelinedComputeGraphicsScheduleEvidence and diagnose_pipelined_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness so first-party diagnostics can classify graphics consuming a previous output slot while compute writes a different current slot as backend-private timing-ready rather than a same-frame serial dependency. RHI D3D12 Per-Queue Fence Synchronization v1 completed first-party FenceValue queue identity and backend-private per-queue D3D12 fence timelines. Vulkan async/performance work, Metal parity, graphics morph+skin composition, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain follow-up work; rhi-d3d12-queue-timestamp-measurement-foundation-v1 is the active RHI timing slice for backend-private graphics/compute timestamp interval diagnostics.

gameCodeGuidance.currentObservability: MK_core provides dependency-free DiagnosticsRecorder, DiagnosticEvent, CounterSample, ProfileSample, ManualProfileClock, SteadyProfileClock, ScopedProfileZone, summarize_diagnostics, DiagnosticsTraceExportOptions, export_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsTraceImportReview, DiagnosticsTraceImportResult, review_diagnostics_trace_json, import_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsOpsHostStatus, DiagnosticsOpsPlanOptions, DiagnosticsOpsArtifact, DiagnosticsOpsPlan, and build_diagnostics_ops_plan for deterministic CPU diagnostics, counters, profile samples, summary aggregation, Trace Event JSON subset export/review/reconstruction, and operations handoff rows without runtime, renderer, platform, SDL3, editor, OS, GPU, network upload, or Dear ImGui dependencies. build_diagnostics_ops_plan reports ready diagnostic-summary and trace-event-json artifacts, a host_gated crash_dump_review row until Debugging Tools for Windows are available, and unsupported telemetry_upload without a caller-provided backend. review_diagnostics_trace_json structurally reviews pasted supported Trace Event JSON and classifies M/i/C/X/unknown phases; import_diagnostics_trace_json reconstructs only first-party exported M/i/C/X Trace Event JSON fields into DiagnosticCapture rows and reports deterministic diagnostics for unsupported reconstruction fields. MK_runtime_host provides the first host adapter: DesktopHostServices can accept a DiagnosticsRecorder plus optional IProfileClock, and DesktopGameRunner records runtime_host.frame profile samples plus renderer.frames_started, renderer.frames_finished, renderer.sprites_submitted, and renderer.meshes_submitted counters from IRenderer::stats() without exposing native/backend details. MK_rhi exposes backend-neutral GPU debug scope/marker hooks with printable-label validation, RhiStats counters, D3D12 PIX-friendly command-list events, null/Vulkan count-only marker paths, D3D12 graphics-queue timestamp frequency reporting, and read-only IRhiDevice memory_diagnostics snapshots for DXGI video-memory data or descriptor-byte estimates. Native dump writing, crash-report backends, telemetry SDK/upload execution, symbol publication, production allocator hooks/enforcement, timestamp query capture, Vulkan/Metal native debug-label parity, broader editor native save/open dialogs outside Profiler, arbitrary JSON conversion beyond the first-party exported Trace Event JSON subset, and flame graph UI remain follow-up adapters.

MK_ui_renderer provides the first concrete engine-owned renderer adapter for converting styled RendererBox payloads into IRenderer SpriteCommand submissions through UiRendererTheme and submit_ui_renderer_submission, for resolving deterministic monospace glyph rows through UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette / UiRendererGlyphAtlasBinding into atlas-backed text glyph sprite submissions, for building glyph atlas palette rows from RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph package metadata through build_ui_renderer_glyph_atlas_palette_from_runtime_ui_atlas, and for resolving image placeholder rows through UiRendererImagePalette / UiRendererImageBinding into image sprite submissions through IRenderer with deterministic resource_id-first and asset_uri-fallback lookup. It reports text adapter row, text glyph availability/resolution/missing/submission, image placeholder, image sprite submission, missing glyph/image resource, accessibility node, and adapter diagnostic availability through the existing MK_renderer public API without making MK_ui depend on renderer code. Generated games must not use Dear ImGui, SDL3, editor modules, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs for HUDs or menus. Installed SDL3 D3D12 package-visible postprocess depth and fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF directional shadow filtering are validated above the backend through private DXIL shader artifacts; hardware comparison samplers, production shadow quality, and Metal depth remain follow-up work. Sampled depth textures are implemented through private typeless resources, D24 DSV views, R24 SRV views, explicit depth_write-to-shader_read transition validation, sampled descriptor writes, D3D12 sampled-depth readback proof, D3D12 depth-aware postprocess readback proof, D3D12 directional shadow receiver readback proof, and D3D12 fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF boundary readback proof.

gameCodeGuidance.currentVulkanRuntimeOwners: Vulkan runtime owners now include VulkanRuntimeInstance, VulkanRuntimeDevice, VulkanRuntimeCommandPool, VulkanRuntimeBuffer, VulkanRuntimeTexture, VulkanRuntimeShaderModule, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSetLayout, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSet, VulkanRuntimePipelineLayout, VulkanRuntimeGraphicsPipeline, VulkanRuntimeComputePipeline, VulkanRuntimeSwapchain, VulkanRuntimeFrameSync, VulkanRuntimeReadbackBuffer, and VulkanRuntimeSampler. The create_rhi_device bridge exposes backend-neutral BufferHandle, TextureHandle, ShaderHandle, DescriptorSetLayoutHandle, DescriptorSetHandle, PipelineLayoutHandle, GraphicsPipelineHandle, ComputePipelineHandle, SwapchainHandle, SwapchainFrameHandle, graphics command lists, immediate FenceValue stats/waits, upload-buffer writes, descriptor updates for uniform/storage buffer, sampled texture, and sampler bindings, standalone texture transitions, buffer copies, buffer-to-texture copies, texture-to-buffer copies, standalone texture render-target clears, optional depth attachment clears, standalone texture render-target draws with optional depth attachments, copy-only submit, command-list-local texture state tracking, stale texture-state submit rejection, submit-success-only texture-state commits, and transient lease creation while keeping VkBuffer, VkImage, VkDeviceMemory, VkShaderModule, VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, VkDescriptorImageInfo, VkSampler, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImageView, VkSemaphore, VkFence, and native commands private. Descriptor creation, update, and binding use create_runtime_descriptor_set_layout, create_runtime_descriptor_set, update_runtime_descriptor_set, descriptor-aware create_runtime_pipeline_layout, and record_runtime_descriptor_set_binding while VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, vkUpdateDescriptorSets, vkCmdBindDescriptorSets, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Pipeline creation uses create_runtime_pipeline_layout, create_runtime_graphics_pipeline, and create_runtime_compute_pipeline with dynamic rendering metadata, native DepthStencilStateDesc mapping, and compute shader modules while VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkPipelineVertexInputStateCreateInfo, VkGraphicsPipelineCreateInfo, VkComputePipelineCreateInfo, vertex input descriptions, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Swapchain creation uses create_runtime_swapchain while VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImage, VkImageView, and image-view destruction order remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Frame sync, acquire, and present use create_runtime_frame_sync, acquire_next_runtime_swapchain_image, and present_runtime_swapchain_image while VkSemaphore, VkFence, VkPresentInfoKHR, vkAcquireNextImageKHR/vkQueuePresentKHR results, and acquired/presented image indices remain private or first-party mapped; the IRhiDevice bridge records shader-free swapchain clear render passes, queues present, submits through private synchronization2, and completes frame ownership on submit. Dynamic rendering color/depth clear and draw recording use record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_clear, record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_draw, record_runtime_texture_rendering_clear, and record_runtime_texture_rendering_draw while VkRenderingInfo, VkRenderingAttachmentInfo including pDepthAttachment, VkCommandBuffer, VkPipeline, VkImageView, viewport/scissor state, vertex/index buffers, and vkCmdBeginRendering/vkCmdBindPipeline/vkCmdBindVertexBuffers/vkCmdBindIndexBuffer/vkCmdDraw/vkCmdDrawIndexed/vkCmdEndRendering function pointers remain private. Standalone texture transition/copy recording uses build_texture_transition_barrier, record_runtime_texture_barrier, record_runtime_buffer_copy, record_runtime_buffer_texture_copy, and record_runtime_texture_buffer_copy while shader-read hazards, depth_write hazards, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, usage/format/state compatibility checks, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkDependencyInfo, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, vkCmdCopyBuffer, vkCmdCopyBufferToImage, vkCmdCopyImageToBuffer, and image layouts remain private or first-party mapped. Synchronization2 recording uses record_runtime_swapchain_frame_barrier and submit_runtime_command_buffer while VkDependencyInfo, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkSubmitInfo2, VkSemaphoreSubmitInfo, VkCommandBufferSubmitInfo, queue handles, semaphores, fences, and raw VkResult values remain private or first-party mapped; requested queue-idle waits must succeed before submit_runtime_command_buffer reports submitted, and logical device creation requires the synchronization2 feature before those commands are requested. Upload and readback use write_runtime_buffer, read_runtime_buffer, create_runtime_readback_buffer, record_runtime_swapchain_image_readback, and read_runtime_readback_buffer while VkBuffer, VkDeviceMemory, VkMemoryRequirements, VkPhysicalDeviceMemoryProperties, VkBufferImageCopy, mapped pointers, and native memory commands remain private or first-party mapped. Environment-gated visible draw/readback, raw texture-sampling, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, shadow receiver readback, RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling, and cooked runtime scene GPU palette proofs now run through IRhiDevice when MK_VULKAN_TEST_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV, or MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts and the local Vulkan runtime is available; default generated-game availability remains gated by runtime and shader-toolchain readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentMetal: SDK-independent MK_rhi_metal scaffold exposes backend identity, host support, capability profile, probe plan, deterministic probe result helpers, non-empty metallib shader library artifact validation, runtime/default-device/command-queue/shader-library readiness planning, texture synchronization planning for render/blit/present encoder intent, platform availability diagnostics, and Apple-only native default-device, command-queue, command-buffer, render-encoder, texture-target, CAMetalLayer drawable acquisition, drawable render-encoder creation, drawable present scheduling, command-buffer submission, and texture-readback ownership through retained private PIMPL objects without including Metal or QuartzCore headers or exposing native handles. gameCodeGuidance.currentInput: VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput with PointerSample/PointerState for mouse, touch, and pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput with GamepadButton/GamepadAxis state for gamepad buttons and axes, mobile touch sample mapping into first-party PointerSample values without native handles, VirtualPointerInput::touch_gesture for active touch count, centroid, centroid delta, and two-touch pinch scale primitives, TouchGestureRecognizer for tap, double-tap, long-press, pan, swipe, pinch, rotate, and canceled gesture events, and MK_runtime RuntimeInputActionMap plus RuntimeInputStateView and RuntimeInputContextStack for deterministic GameEngine.RuntimeInputActions.v4 keyboard, pointer, gamepad button, scalar key-pair, and scalar gamepad-axis action documents evaluated over first-party virtual input state through bind_key, bind_pointer, bind_gamepad_button, bind_key_axis, bind_gamepad_axis, bind_key_in_context, bind_pointer_in_context, bind_gamepad_button_in_context, bind_key_axis_in_context, bind_gamepad_axis_in_context, context-aware action_down, and context-aware axis_value. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action to turn the next allowed first-party key, pointer, or gamepad-button press into a validated RuntimeInputRebindingProfile action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis to capture the first deterministic gamepad axis magnitude past a deadzone into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus when a host-owned menu has armed one capture row and needs deterministic focus_retained plus gameplay_input_consumed output for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected candidates. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel rows from make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation when a runtime/game menu needs reviewed base/profile action and axis display labels plus symbolic glyph lookup keys. Empty context stacks use default; non-empty stacks select the first context that declares the requested action. Scalar action axes apply deterministic clamp/deadzone handling; broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, full runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, SDL3, and native device handles remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputRebindingProfiles: MK_runtime RuntimeInputRebindingProfile and GameEngine.RuntimeInputRebindingProfile.v1 provide deterministic persisted full-row action and axis overlays over a caller-supplied base RuntimeInputActionMap. Use validate_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, apply_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, serialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, deserialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, load_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, and write_runtime_input_rebinding_profile through IFileSystem/RootedFileSystem. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action for host-independent digital action trigger capture from first-party keyboard, pointer, and gamepad-button state into a validated action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis for host-independent gamepad-axis and two-held-keyboard key-pair axis capture (capture_keyboard_key_pair_axes) from first-party VirtualGamepadInput axis magnitudes and VirtualInput key-down pairs into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus for an armed/focused/modal capture guard that reports focus_retained while waiting and gameplay_input_consumed for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected digital candidates after the guard passes. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationToken, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationRow, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel, present_runtime_input_action_trigger, present_runtime_input_axis_source, and make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation for host-independent display rows with deterministic labels and symbolic glyph lookup keys after loading, validating, or applying a profile. Visible full interactive runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, SDL3 input APIs, editor-private runtime APIs, native device handles, and cloud/binary saves remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentProcessExecution: ProcessCommand, RecordingProcessRunner, run_process_command, RootedFileSystem, and Windows Win32ProcessRunner for shell-free executable-plus-arguments process execution with stdout/stderr capture and rooted project file access
gameCodeGuidance.currentToolExecution: ShaderToolProcessRunner and ShaderToolExecutionPolicy bridge shader compile commands to platform process execution with artifact output-root policy and reviewed executable override for PATH names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths; ShaderArtifactValidationProcessRunner bridges spirv-val validation commands to the same shell-free process policy; DXC Vulkan SPIR-V command planning uses -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3 through executable-plus-argument vectors; MK_editor_core make_viewport_shader_compile_requests queues default D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V viewport shader artifacts from one HLSL source, while make_material_preview_shader_compile_requests queues factor-only and base-color-textured material preview D3D12 DXIL/Vulkan SPIR-V artifacts from a material-preview HLSL source; discover_shader_tools finds deterministic dxc, spirv-val, metal, and metallib descriptors and tracks dxc.spirv-codegen capability markers for deterministic toolchain roots; evaluate_shader_toolchain_readiness reports D3D12 DXIL, Vulkan SPIR-V, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and Metal library readiness; editor ProjectShaderToolSettings executable values may be reviewed PATH tool names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths while working directory, artifact root, and cache index paths stay project-relative; make_spirv_shader_validation_command and execute_shader_artifact_validation_action provide reviewed SPIR-V artifact validation before native Vulkan shader module promotion; MK_editor_core ShaderToolDiscoveryState exposes discovered tools and readiness diagnostics to editor UI/AI workflows; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 reports local shader tool blockers, reads Process/User/Machine environment state, searches MK_SHADER_TOOLCHAIN_ROOT, User/Machine PATH, Vulkan SDK variables, and vcpkg tool roots before Windows SDK fallback, runs a real quoted DXC SPIR-V CodeGen probe, and tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 -RequireAll can gate CI lanes; MK_editor_core ViewportShaderArtifactState verifies non-empty compiled D3D12 VS/PS DXIL bytecode and Vulkan VS/PS SPIR-V artifacts before native viewport promotion and can query readiness by shader id for material preview variants; build_shader_artifact_provenance records compiler, command, artifact, and input fingerprints for cache invalidation; ShaderArtifactCacheIndex and execute_shader_compile_action provide persistent shader cache index storage, cache-hit reuse, .geprovenance writes, deterministic test artifact marker writes, and real artifact enforcement for compiler-backed runs; build_shader_hot_reload_plan and build_shader_pipeline_recreation_plan turn stale provenance or successful non-cached compiles into explicit pipeline recreation decisions; build_shader_pipeline_cache_plan batches selected compile requests into deterministic hot-reload, compile-required, pipeline-recreation, cache-entry-current, and cache-index-repair rows; reconcile_shader_pipeline_cache_index repairs selected first-party cache indexes from current provenance without invoking compilers or writing artifacts; execute_asset_import_plan runs asset import or recook plans through IFileSystem, decodes first-party texture/mesh/audio/material source documents including optional hex byte payloads, optionally routes PNG, glTF, and common audio sources through MK_tools-owned asset-importers adapters when MK_ENABLE_ASSET_IMPORTERS is enabled, writes deterministic cooked artifacts preserving first-party byte payloads, decoded PNG RGBA8 payloads, decoded common-audio PCM payloads, and glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved mesh byte payloads for embedded/loaded buffers while rejecting partial normal/UV/tangent-frame primitives; execute_asset_runtime_recook builds recook plans, executes imports transactionally, stages successful cooked artifacts through AssetRuntimeReplacementState, and rolls every requested asset back on recook transaction failure; scan_asset_files_for_hot_reload turns registered asset files into deterministic hot reload snapshots; PollingFileWatcher turns IFileSystem roots into deterministic added, modified, and removed file events for AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler inputs; WindowsFileWatcher turns Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW notifications into FileWatchEvent records; LinuxFileWatcher turns inotify notifications into FileWatchEvent records; MacOSFileWatcher turns FSEvents notifications into FileWatchEvent records; native watcher handles stay backend-private and host-unavailable backends report explicit diagnostics
gameCodeGuidance.currentAssets: AssetRegistry, AssetDependencyGraph, AssetHotReloadTracker, AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler for debounce-based source and dependency-invalidated recook requests, AssetHotReloadApplyState for failed-reload rollback state, AssetRuntimeReplacementState for staging runtime asset replacements until a safe point and rolling back failed recooks without losing the active asset, AssetCookedPackageIndex with content hashes, dependency edges, serialization, and deterministic recook decisions, deterministic GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture package edges while native GPU sprite batching, package-authored atlas metadata workflows beyond the reviewed decode/pack path, and production atlas editor UX remain follow-up, ShaderMetadataRegistry with descriptor reflection rows, shader compile command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, AssetImportMetadataRegistry, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document serialization with optional hex byte payloads, build_asset_import_plan, build_asset_recook_plan, MaterialDefinition, MaterialInstanceDefinition, deterministic material/material-instance serialization, material instance composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, and explicit MaterialPipelineBindingMetadata for backend-neutral descriptor/pipeline layout derivation with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and sampler rows; optional PNG/glTF/common-audio source import adapters are implemented in MK_tools behind the asset-importers feature and feed this first-party cooked-artifact path; MK_tools preserves first-party texture data_hex, mesh vertex/index data_hex, and audio data_hex into deterministic cooked artifacts, decodes PNG RGBA8 payloads into texture data_hex, decodes common-audio PCM samples into audio data_hex, and cooks glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved accessors plus indexed/generated indices into deterministic mesh.vertex_data_hex and uint32 mesh.index_data_hex for embedded/loaded buffers; MK_runtime now reads cooked package indexes through IFileSystem, validates runtime payload hashes and dependencies, exposes first-party runtime asset handles, provides typed cooked texture/mesh/audio/material/scene/tilemap payload access including decoded optional byte payloads, serialized scene text, tilemap atlas texture path checks, and tilemap_texture edge inspection, resolves scene material dependencies through resolve_runtime_scene_materials, stages all-or-nothing package replacement at safe points, owns host-independent save/settings/localization/input action session documents, and exposes RuntimeDiagnosticReport helpers for package load, payload, scene, and session failures; MK_runtime_rhi creates backend-neutral RHI texture resources, mesh vertex/index buffer upload paths with derived position-only stride 12 or lit tangent-space stride 48 metadata, 256-byte-backed material factor uniform buffers with 64-byte factor payloads, sampler-aware owner-device-validated material descriptor bindings, and submitted_fence rows from typed runtime payload uploads; MK_runtime_scene_rhi turns cooked package scene packets into retained SceneGpuBindingPalette resources through those typed runtime payload parsers and RHI upload helpers, preserves actual submit-order submitted_upload_fences rows, and reports submitted_upload_fence_count plus last_submitted_upload_fence rows; MK_editor_core can turn selected cooked material/texture artifacts into a non-throwing GPU binding preview plan using typed runtime payload parsers; and renderer MeshCommand bindings can now consume those RHI handles for descriptor binding, vertex/index binding, and indexed draws through SceneGpuBindingPalette
gameCodeGuidance.currentAnimation: FloatKeyframe, Vec3Keyframe, FloatAnimationTrack, sorted keyframe validation, clamped linear keyframe sampling, sorted float curve binding samples through sample_float_animation_tracks, sampled scalar curve application to caller-owned Transform3D rows through AnimationTransformCurveBinding, AnimationTransformComponent, and apply_float_animation_samples_to_transform3d, little-endian cooked float clip byte-row conversion through FloatAnimationTrackByteSource, make_float_animation_track_from_f32_bytes, and AnimationSkeletonJointDesc, JointLocalTransform, AnimationPose, AnimationJointTrackDesc, validate_animation_skeleton_desc, validate_animation_pose, validate_animation_joint_tracks, Application v1 through QuatKeyframe, QuatAnimationTrack, is_valid_quat_keyframes, sample_quat_keyframes, AnimationJointTrack3dDesc, validate_animation_joint_tracks_3d, and Normal Stream v1, Animation CPU Skinned Tangent Stream v1, and Animation CPU Skinned Bitangent Handedness v1 through AnimationCpuSkinningDesc optional bind_normals/bind_tangents/bind_tangent_handedness, AnimationCpuSkinnedVertexStream positions/normals/tangents/bitangents, AnimationCpuSkinningDiagnostic, validate_animation_cpu_skinning_desc, is_valid_animation_cpu_skinning_desc, and skin_animation_vertices_cpu, plus Animation Morph POSITION+NORMAL+TANGENT CPU v0 through AnimationMorphMeshCpuDesc, AnimationMorphTargetCpuDesc, validate_animation_morph_mesh_cpu_desc, is_valid_animation_morph_mesh_cpu_desc, apply_animation_morph_targets_positions_cpu, apply_animation_morph_targets_normals_cpu, and apply_animation_morph_targets_tangents_cpu, AnimationMorphWeightsTrackDesc with validate_animation_morph_weights_track_desc and sample_animation_morph_weights_at_time, planar two-bone IK via AnimationTwoBoneIkXyBendSide, AnimationTwoBoneIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, and solve_animation_two_bone_ik_xy_plane for explicit bend-side plus finite non-collinear pole-vector branch selection, analytic two-bone 3D orientation basis output via AnimationTwoBoneIk3dDesc, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dJointFrame, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dSolution, and solve_animation_two_bone_ik_3d_orientation, and deterministic multi-segment XY FABRIK chain IK via AnimationFabrikIkXyBendSide, AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::bend_side, AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, AnimationFabrikIkXySegmentRotationLimit, AnimationFabrikIkXySolution, solve_animation_fabrik_ik_xy_chain, AnimationIkLocalRotationLimit, and apply_animation_fabrik_ik_xy_solution_to_pose for optional explicit bend-side plus finite non-collinear pole-vector branch selection, solver-integrated segment local Z limits, signed-pi local angle normalization, and local pose write-back clamps, plus deterministic multi-segment 3D FABRIK chain IK via AnimationFabrikIk3dDesc, AnimationFabrikIk3dSolution, and solve_animation_fabrik_ik_3d_chain for root-fixed 3D joint positions, and apply_animation_fabrik_ik_3d_solution_to_pose for validated AnimationPose3d unit-quaternion local rotation write-back, plus Animation 3D Local Rotation Limits Twist Controls v1 through AnimationIkLocalRotationLimit3d, validate_animation_ik_local_rotation_limits_3d, apply_animation_local_rotation_limits_3d, and the local-limit overload of apply_animation_fabrik_ik_3d_solution_to_pose for deterministic swing/twist clamps and constraint reporting in AnimationPose3d, plus Animation Root Motion Foundation v1 through AnimationRootMotionSampleDesc, AnimationRootMotionSample, AnimationRootMotionAccumulationDesc, AnimationRootMotionAccumulation, AnimationRootMotionDiagnostic, validate_animation_root_motion_sample, is_valid_animation_root_motion_sample, sample_animation_root_motion, validate_animation_root_motion_accumulation, is_valid_animation_root_motion_accumulation, and accumulate_animation_root_motion for root-joint translation and Z-rotation start/end/delta sampling plus explicit-duration forward loop accumulation. MK_assets provides authored GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource.v1 binding source rows through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument for mapping scalar curve targets to named transform components. MK_tools can derive those rows from glTF node TRS targets through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_binding_source using glTF node names or deterministic fallback names, and can import full unit-quaternion 3D node transform tracks through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d and bridge them into cooked quaternion clip source rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. MK_assets exposes GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 and GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip.v1 for selected 3D TRS tracks, and MK_runtime exposes runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload for cooked package records. MK_runtime_scene resolves scalar binding rows for RuntimeSceneInstance data through resolve_runtime_scene_animation_transform_bindings, can apply caller-provided FloatAnimationCurveSample rows through apply_runtime_scene_animation_transform_samples, and can apply validated AnimationPose3d rows to named RuntimeSceneInstance nodes through apply_runtime_scene_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can sample a cooked scalar AnimationFloatClipSourceDocument and apply AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance data through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_float_clip, then rebuild the render packet for generated 3D package smoke validation. MK_scene_renderer can also sample AnimationJointTrack3dDesc rows, apply named-joint AnimationPose3d rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance scene nodes, and rebuild the render packet through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can also sample cooked scalar morph-weight clips against RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload rows through sample_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_animation_float_clip and return deterministic CPU-morphed positions for generated 3D package smoke validation. Generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and sample_desktop_runtime_game can load cooked animation_quaternion_clip records through runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload, decode them with make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes, and validate --require-quaternion-animation through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d plus quaternion_animation_scene_applied/quaternion_animation_scene_rotation_z counters. JointLocalTransform remains the Z-only local pose contract for existing clips, while AnimationJointLocalTransform3d covers translation, positive scale, and unit-quaternion local rotations for 3D model-pose construction; AnimationJointTrack3dDesc can sample selected translation, quaternion rotation, and positive-scale tracks into AnimationPose3d with rest-pose fallback for missing components, and AnimationJointTrack3dByteSource plus make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes decode cooked little-endian component byte rows into those descriptors. MK_tools can import LINEAR glTF node TRS 3D rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d with full unit-quaternion rotation keyframes for caller-mapped AnimationPose3d workflows and can emit GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. Backward root-motion accumulation, negative-time wrapping, inferred clip duration, tangent-space material/render integration beyond the cooked tangent-frame schema, broader GPU skinning parity, broader IK constraints beyond the implemented planar segment local Z and 3D swing/twist local clamps, authored IK assets, and animation graph authoring are not implemented yet.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAudio: AudioMixer with logical buses, voices, asset-id clip references, clip metadata registration, deterministic mix plans, spatial_mix_plan for listener/source distance attenuation and stereo pan metadata, AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state, sample-frame render scheduling, streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, underrun diagnostics, AudioClipSampleData validation, selectable nearest/linear resampling quality, and render_interleaved_float output with gain, channel conversion, and clamped interleaved float PCM. AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float provide the device-independent stream pump baseline: host adapters report current queued frames and a target, MK_audio schedules bounded render chunks at device_frame + queued_frames, returns no_work when the queue is already full enough, and rejects invalid formats, invalid queue/render budgets, invalid resampling quality, and device-frame overflow. Optional MK_audio_sdl3 SdlAudioRuntime/SdlAudioDevice opens SDL3 default playback streams through SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream, queues silence or rendered interleaved float PCM, reports queued frames, and exposes pause/resume/clear controls without public SDL handles; platform/android contains a private AAudio output adapter that opens a low-latency float32 output stream, uses an atomic FIFO for callback-safe sample transfer, and starts/stops with Android lifecycle events without public AAudio handles; codecs, streaming decode integration, HRTF, DSP graph execution, device hotplug/selection, and mixer authoring remain follow-up work

PhysicsJoint3DDiagnostic, PhysicsDistanceJoint3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DConfig, PhysicsJointSolve3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DRow, PhysicsJointSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_joints_3d, and Dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, vehicles, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends outside the 1.0 ready surface, middleware, oriented boxes, mesh/convex casts, timing benchmarks, and broad physics beyond this first-party 1.0 ready surface are not implemented yet
gameCodeGuidance.currentNavigation: NavigationGrid, NavigationGridCoord, NavigationPathRequest, NavigationPathResult, NavigationPathStatus, and find_navigation_path provide a standard-library-only deterministic cardinal-grid A* foundation with walkability, positive traversal costs, stable tie-breaking, invalid/blocked endpoint diagnostics, no-path reporting, total path cost, and visited-node counts. NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering provide Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 for deterministic grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit target cursor advancement, max-step and arrival-radius movement, advanced/remaining distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering desired velocity/speed. NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent provide Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 for deterministic value-type agent movement state/ticks with idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid statuses, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, remaining-distance reporting, and desired velocity from actual path movement. NavigationGridPathValidationRequest, NavigationGridPathValidationResult, NavigationGridPathValidationStatus, validate_navigation_grid_path, NavigationGridReplanRequest, NavigationGridReplanResult, NavigationGridReplanStatus, and replan_navigation_grid_path provide Grid Dynamic Obstacle Replan v0 for validating remaining cardinal-grid paths against the current grid, reporting empty/start/goal/out-of-bounds/blocked/non-cardinal/unsupported-adjacency/cost-overflow diagnostics, reusing valid remaining paths, and recalculating deterministic replacement paths around newly blocked cells through find_navigation_path. NavigationLocalAvoidanceRequest, NavigationLocalAvoidanceResult, NavigationLocalAvoidanceDiagnostic, and calculate_navigation_local_avoidance provide Navigation Agent Local Avoidance v0 for finite-input single-agent desired velocity adjustment against explicit nearby neighbor rows, duplicate-neighbor and calculation-overflow diagnostics, deterministic neighbor ordering, closest-approach prediction over the requested time window, overlap/future-collision separation, applied-neighbor counts, and max-speed clamping. NavigationGridPathSmoothingRequest, NavigationGridPathSmoothingResult, NavigationGridPathSmoothingStatus, and smooth_navigation_grid_path provide Navigation Grid Path Smoothing v0 for reducing validated cardinal grid paths through deterministic walkable line-of-sight while preserving start and goal waypoints. NavigationGridAgentPathRequest, NavigationGridAgentPathPlan, NavigationGridAgentPathStatus, NavigationGridAgentPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_grid_agent_path provide Navigation Production Path v1 by composing pathfinding, optional smoothing, point-path mapping, diagnostics, point-count/cost evidence, and NavigationAgentState creation for generated-game route setup. Generated games can use these for tile/grid movement and headless AI tests. navmesh assets, polygon navigation, spline/funnel smoothing, full crowd simulation, RVO/ORCA, scene/physics integration, editor visualization, middleware, and background navigation jobs are not implemented yet.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAi: BehaviorTreeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeKind, BehaviorTreeLeafResult, BehaviorTreeStatus, BehaviorTreeDiagnostic, BehaviorTreeTickResult, BehaviorTreeBlackboardValue, BehaviorTreeBlackboard, BehaviorTreeBlackboardCondition, BehaviorTreeEvaluationContext, and evaluate_behavior_tree provide Behavior Tree Foundation v0 plus Behavior Tree Blackboard Conditions v0 for deterministic memoryless sequence, selector, action, and condition evaluation. AiPerceptionAgent2D, AiPerceptionTarget2D, AiPerceptionSnapshot2D, build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, and write_ai_perception_blackboard provide AI Perception Services v1 for explicit caller-supplied 2D sight/hearing classification, stable primary-target selection, duplicate target rejection, finite validation, and blackboard projection into caller-selected fact keys. Action leaves are game-owned and supplied as explicit node-id statuses; condition leaves can either use legacy game-supplied leaf statuses or typed blackboard bindings over bool, signed integer, finite double, and string facts. The evaluator and perception projection own validation, stable traversal/order, status propagation, visited-node traces, and missing-root, duplicate-node-id, missing-child, empty-composite, leaf-has-children, missing-leaf-result, invalid-leaf-result, duplicate-leaf-result, duplicate-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-value, duplicate-blackboard-condition, invalid-blackboard-condition, missing-blackboard-key, blackboard-type-mismatch, invalid-blackboard-operator, invalid-agent, invalid-target, duplicate-target-id, invalid-perception-blackboard-key, calculation-overflow, cycle, and max-depth diagnostics. sample_ai_navigation proves that generated game code can compose perception projection and this evaluator with MK_navigation plan_navigation_grid_agent_path output and NavigationAgentState ticks without adding engine coupling. sample_gameplay_foundation proves a broader headless source-tree runtime composition loop over build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, write_ai_perception_blackboard, evaluate_behavior_tree, plan_navigation_grid_agent_path, build_physics_world_3d_from_authored_collision_scene, move_physics_character_controller_3d, render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float, and MK_animation state transitions through public APIs only. Async tasks, decorators/services, utility AI, scene/physics/navigation engine integration, persistent blackboard services, middleware, scripting, and editor graph authoring are not implemented yet.

Generated game code can stay on public mirakana::GameApp, SdlDesktopGameHost, DesktopGameRunner, IWindow, VirtualInput, VirtualPointerInput, VirtualGamepadInput, VirtualLifecycle, IFileSystem/RootedFileSystem, IRenderer, and MK_core diagnostics contracts; swapchain-backed RhiFrameRenderer resize, presentation reports, scene GPU binding diagnostics, postprocess/framegraph diagnostics, and runtime-host observability capture are host-driven through those public contracts.

Android Release Device Matrix v1 also keeps Android-unsupported floating-point std::from_chars out of first-party metadata/session parsers by using portable classic-locale parsing. platform/ios contains a CMake/Xcode UIKit/Metal bundle template with Metal-backed root view setup, Application Support/Caches/Documents storage root mapping, game manifest resources, bundle identifier inputs, simulator/device SDK selection, simulator signing suppression, Xcode signing inputs, iOS Simulator install/launch smoke scripting, and a dedicated macOS GitHub Actions validation lane. pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1 validates template presence and reports Android SDK/NDK/JDK/Gradle/signing/AVD/device or Apple full-Xcode/iOS-SDK/iOS-runtime/signing blockers explicitly. pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-apple-host-evidence.ps1 records Apple Metal iOS Host Evidence v1 rows for macOS/full-Xcode, xcrun, iOS Simulator SDK/runtime, Xcode-resolved metal/metallib, signing configuration, and iOS/macOS workflow coverage; this Windows host reports apple-host-evidence-check: host-gated. steps: Write a concise game spec before implementation.
Use only public mirakana:: APIs from engine headers.

Use MK_core diagnostics/profile/trace export/review/reconstruction APIs for deterministic CPU observability; host adapters can attach a DiagnosticsRecorder and optional IProfileClock through DesktopHostServices to capture runtime_host.frame and renderer counters; use build_diagnostics_ops_plan plus export_diagnostics_trace_json, review_diagnostics_trace_json, and import_diagnostics_trace_json for dependency-free summary/trace/crash-review/telemetry handoff and first-party exported Trace Event JSON review/reconstruction instead of inventing game-local profiling formats, and do not expose native/backend handles.
Do not use Dear ImGui, SDL3, editor, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs for game runtime HUDs or menus; target first-party mirakana::ui contracts.

When authored source assets and Scene v2 data need a host-independent runtime scene package, use the validated authored-to-runtime workflow register-source-asset -> cook-registered-source-assets -> migrate-scene-v2-runtime-package -> mirakana::runtime::load_runtime_asset_package -> mirakana::runtime_scene::instantiate_runtime_scene. Select validate-runtime-scene-package inputs from game.agent.json runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows after the package exists, and stop there unless a selected renderer or desktop recipe adds its own host-gated validation.

Register headless/default games with MK_add_game in games/CMakeLists.txt; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimePackage for config-only desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage for first-party cooked-scene desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime2DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 2D gameplay with cooked sprite/material/audio/orthographic scene payloads, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime3DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 3D gameplay with cooked texture/mesh/morph_mesh_cpu/material/animation_float_clip/animation_quaternion_clip/perspective scene/physics-collision payloads plus transform/morph/quaternion scene-transform package smoke, selected safe-point package streaming smoke, selected renderer quality package smoke, selected scene collision package smoke, and primary camera/controller package smoke, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage only when a scaffold needs source material/HLSL authoring inputs plus host-built desktop shader artifacts, or MK_add_desktop_runtime_game with GAME_MANIFEST plus mirakana::SdlDesktopGameHost only when selecting the optional desktop runtime lane, and declare config/assets/cooked package payloads in runtimePackageFiles with PACKAGE_FILES_FROM_MANIFEST so they ship beside the executable. Cooked-scene, packaged-2D, and packaged-3D manifests must also declare runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows with safe game-relative .geindex paths and explicit scene asset keys for validate-runtime-scene-package, plus host-gated-safe-point packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows that reference those validation targets and .geindex package files for selected safe-point package streaming execution. Source-tree desktop runtime builds stage registered package files and shader artifacts beside a target-specific executable directory to avoid cross-target generated payload collisions, and generated 2D/material-shader/3D package targets get DXC-available source-tree shader-artifact CTests before installed package validation when they declare selected D3D12 shader artifacts. Source material and HLSL files remain authoring inputs and must not be listed in runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, or packageStreamingResidencyTargets. Use the editor Apply Package Registration button or tools/register-runtime-package-files.ps1 -GameManifest games/<game_name>/game.agent.json -RuntimePackageFile <game-relative-file> after reviewing package draft rows, then run schema/package validation. Use literal CMake PACKAGE_FILES only when intentionally mirroring the manifest.

Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/prepare-worktree.ps1 after entering a manual linked worktree so ignored worktree roots and external/vcpkg are ready before configure. After a task-owned PR is merged into main, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> -BaseRef origin/main -BaseBranch main -Remote origin -DeleteLocalBranch from Local or another non-target checkout for guarded post-merge cleanup; it fetches/prunes origin, requires the local checkout to be clean and on the base branch, fast-forwards it to origin/main, then removes only clean merged worktrees. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-toolchain.ps1 when diagnosing local CMake, CTest, CPack, clang-format, Visual Studio, MSBuild, or PATH issues; toolchain-check also enforces that visible CMake configure/build presets inherit normalized-configure-environment / normalized-build-environment so repository wrappers collapse parent PATH/Path variants into one child PATH for CMake/MSBuild, and reports linked-worktree vcpkg readiness; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-format.ps1 / pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/format.ps1 for repository formatting because raw clang-format --dry-run commands require direct-clang-format-status=ready or an explicit resolved tool path, and those wrappers also check/apply tracked text normalization (LF, UTF-8 without BOM, a single final newline, and no trailing EOF blank lines); use tools/check-text-format.ps1 / tools/format-text.ps1 for text-only loops; run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1 before completion. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1 when the game selects desktop-game-runtime, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 for the default sample-shell desktop-runtime-release lane, run tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget <target> for a registered desktop runtime package target with package smoke metadata and package file validation, run the explicit real-window D3D12 or Vulkan smoke command only on hosts with the matching runtime/toolchain support, or run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/build-gui.ps1 when broader editor/Dear ImGui readiness is needed.
